asi.hypothesis: Since `INITIAL_CACHE_BLOCKS=256` was the strongest stabilizer on the slowed 1024-token lane, it might also improve the 16k explicit-request DFlash lane relative to cache512.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput fell to ~11.30 tok/s, well below the 16k DFlash baseline at cache512 (~14.71) and far below corrected stock (~22.03).
asi.next_action_hint: Do not transfer the 1024-lane cache256 assumption directly to 16k. If cache tuning continues at 16k, prefer bracketing above 512 or combine with a different lever.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, lowering INITIAL_CACHE_BLOCKS from 512 to 256 is strongly harmful on the explicit-request DFlash lane.

description: Cleanly re-run the previously interrupted forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash block-size test at `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=13`. It completed this time and regressed badly.

asi.hypothesis: A smaller fixed speculative block size may improve the forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash lane relative to the current block-15 baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput fell to ~10.78 tok/s, well below the 16k DFlash baseline (~14.71) and far below corrected stock (~22.03).
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue smaller fixed block sizes below 15 on the current 16k explicit DFlash lane; if block-size tuning continues, look upward instead.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=13` is strongly harmful on the explicit-request DFlash lane.

description: Continue the forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash block-size sweep by increasing the fixed speculative block from 15 to `16`. It also regressed badly.

asi.hypothesis: A slightly larger fixed speculative block may improve the forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash lane if long-tail acceptance benefits from fewer verify cycles.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput fell to ~11.15 tok/s, well below the 16k DFlash baseline (~14.71) and far below corrected stock (~22.03).
asi.next_action_hint: The local fixed-block neighborhood around 15 looks peaked already at 16k. Shift to long-tail-only adaptive/gated ideas rather than more nearby fixed block sweeps.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=16` is strongly harmful on the explicit-request DFlash lane.

description: Test a long-tail-only adaptive block path on the forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash lane by enabling adaptive block sizing only after token 8192. This improves over other failed 16k retunes, but is still well below the baseline and stock.

asi.hypothesis: A very-late adaptive block fallback may help the 16k DFlash tail without harming the earlier, healthier portion of decode, unlike earlier always-on or too-early adaptive variants.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput reached only ~12.20 tok/s, still below the 16k DFlash baseline (~14.71) and far below corrected stock (~22.03).
asi.next_action_hint: If adaptive block sizing is revisited at 16k, push it even later or make it more selective; this start point is not enough.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, tail-only adaptive block sizing starting at 8192 is less harmful than earlier adaptive variants, but still not competitive.

description: Refine the long-tail-only adaptive block idea on the forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash lane by delaying the start further to token 12000. It remained well below the baseline and slightly worse than starting at 8192.

asi.hypothesis: If adaptive block resizing only needs to act in the very final tail, starting it later than 8192 may preserve more of the healthy early decode while still helping 16k completion throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput was only ~12.06 tok/s, still below both the 16k DFlash baseline (~14.71) and the already-discarded adaptive-from-8192 result (~12.20).
asi.next_action_hint: Deprioritize the current adaptive-block branch for 16k unless a more selective trigger than a simple token threshold is introduced.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, delaying adaptive block sizing from 8192 to 12000 does not rescue the DFlash tail and is slightly worse.

description: Bracket the forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash cache reservation more finely by raising `INITIAL_CACHE_BLOCKS` from 512 to `768`. It regressed, so the 16k cache sweet spot still points back to 512 rather than upward.

asi.hypothesis: The current 16k explicit-request DFlash baseline may prefer a slightly larger cache reservation than 512, even though 1024 was flat, so 768 could be a better midpoint.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput fell to ~11.82 tok/s, below the 16k DFlash baseline (~14.71) and below corrected stock (~22.03).
asi.next_action_hint: Treat 512 as the working cache-reservation anchor for the 16k explicit lane unless a different interacting lever emerges.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, increasing INITIAL_CACHE_BLOCKS from 512 to 768 is harmful on the explicit-request DFlash lane; the cache bracket still points back to 512.

description: Close the last nearby fixed-block gap on the forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash lane by testing `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=14`. It also regressed badly.

asi.hypothesis: If block 15 is not truly the local optimum at 16k, block 14 could recover some tail performance without the severe regressions seen at 13 or 16.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput fell to ~11.26 tok/s, well below the 16k DFlash baseline (~14.71) and far below corrected stock (~22.03).
asi.next_action_hint: Treat the local fixed-block neighborhood around 15 as exhausted at 16k; shift to a different class of lever rather than more nearby fixed sweeps.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=14` is strongly harmful on the explicit-request DFlash lane, so blocks 13/14/16 all fail relative to the block-15 baseline.

description: Retune the forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash lane for long KV lengths by increasing the split-SDPA chunk size from the default 8 to `16`. This is a major honest win: it lifts 16k DFlash well above the old 14.71 baseline and above corrected 16k stock (~22.03).

asi.hypothesis: The 16k DFlash tail may be dominated by split full-attention chunking overhead rather than the draft/cache knobs already tried; a larger split-SDPA chunk size could materially improve throughput only at long KV lengths.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPLIT_CHUNK_SIZE=16` is a real long-context lever: throughput jumps to ~24.76 tok/s, beating both the old DFlash baseline and corrected stock.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm this 16k split-chunk win with a rerun, then bracket nearby chunk sizes (e.g. 12, 24, 32) before moving up to 32k.

description: Confirmation rerun for the forced-length 16k explicit-request DFlash split-SDPA retune at `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPLIT_CHUNK_SIZE=16`. It repeated stronger and clearly validates the long-context win over stock.

asi.hypothesis: If the 16k split-chunk retune is a real long-context lever rather than noise, a clean rerun should stay above both the old DFlash baseline and corrected stock.
asi.learning: The 16k split-SDPA chunk retune is robust: a confirmation rerun improved further to ~26.14 tok/s, well above corrected stock.
asi.next_action_hint: Bracket nearby split chunk sizes around 16 on the 16k lane, then carry the winning long-context shape upward to 32k.

description: Bracket the new 16k split-SDPA long-context win downward by reducing `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPLIT_CHUNK_SIZE` from 16 to `12` on the explicit-request DFlash lane. It collapsed badly, showing the new win is not a broad plateau.

asi.hypothesis: If the 16k split-SDPA chunk-size improvement is broad rather than sharp, chunk 12 might preserve most of the gain while using smaller work units.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput dropped to ~11.84 tok/s, below the old 16k DFlash baseline (~14.71) and far below both chunk16 keeps and corrected stock (~22.03).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep chunk 16 as the working 16k shape and bracket upward rather than downward next.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, the split-SDPA chunk-size win is sharply peaked: chunk 12 collapses while chunk 16 is a real win.

description: Bracket the new 16k split-SDPA long-context win upward by increasing `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPLIT_CHUNK_SIZE` from 16 to `24` on the explicit-request DFlash lane. It stayed strong and still beat stock, but did not beat the confirmed chunk-16 best.

asi.hypothesis: If the 16k split-SDPA chunk-size win continues upward, chunk 24 might outperform chunk 16 on the long-context explicit DFlash lane.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput was strong at ~24.85 tok/s and still above corrected stock, but it did not exceed the confirmed chunk-16 best (~26.14 tok/s).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep chunk 16 as the 16k winner for now; if bracketing continues, test one larger point (e.g. 32) before moving the winning shape to 32k.
asi.learning: At forced 16k, larger split-SDPA chunks remain a real lever, but chunk 24 is slightly worse than chunk 16.

description: Carry the newly validated 16k long-context winner upward unchanged to establish the first forced-length 32k explicit-request DFlash checkpoint: request-level DFlash, request-driven thinking, cache512, block15, split chunk16. It produced a strong 32k DFlash baseline but is not a keep yet because this workload change still needs a corrected stock comparison.

asi.hypothesis: The same split-SDPA chunk-size retune that rescued forced 16k may transfer upward and provide a competitive forced 32k DFlash baseline on the real server path.
asi.rollback_reason: This establishes a new 32k DFlash checkpoint (~21.10 tok/s), but it is below the current session best metric and cannot be judged as a keep until matched against corrected 32k stock.
asi.next_action_hint: Run the corrected forced-length 32k stock comparison next with DFlash explicitly disabled in both workload env and model settings, then decide whether 32k DFlash is honestly ahead.
asi.learning: The 16k-winning split-SDPA retune transfers surprisingly well to forced 32k, yielding an initial DFlash baseline of ~21.10 tok/s on the real server path.

description: Run the corrected forced-length 32k stock comparison by explicitly disabling DFlash in both workload env and the Qwen model-settings stanza. Corrected stock lands at ~16.27 tok/s, which confirms the carried-up DFlash shape (~21.10 tok/s) is honestly ahead at 32k.

asi.hypothesis: A corrected forced-length 32k stock run is required to tell whether the new long-context DFlash shape is truly competitive or only looks good in isolation.
asi.rollback_reason: This is a stock checkpoint, not a code improvement, so it should not replace the current kept baseline despite being essential for honest comparison.
asi.next_action_hint: Restore the winning DFlash 32k shape and continue upward to 64k or bracket nearby long-context split chunk sizes there.
asi.learning: Corrected forced-length 32k stock is ~16.27 tok/s. The carried-up explicit-request DFlash shape with split chunk16 at ~21.10 tok/s honestly beats stock at 32k.

description: Carry the validated long-context DFlash winner further upward to establish the first forced-length 64k explicit-request checkpoint: request-level DFlash, request-driven thinking, cache512, block15, split chunk16. It holds up surprisingly well at ~21.31 tok/s, but still needs the corrected 64k stock comparison.

asi.hypothesis: If the 16k split-SDPA chunk-size retune is addressing true long-KV overhead, the same explicit DFlash shape may transfer upward to 64k rather than collapsing again.
asi.rollback_reason: This establishes a strong new 64k DFlash checkpoint, but it cannot be treated as a keep until matched against corrected 64k stock on the same real server path.
asi.next_action_hint: Run corrected forced-length 64k stock next with DFlash explicitly disabled in both workload env and model settings.
asi.learning: The current long-context DFlash winner transfers strongly to forced 64k, yielding an initial explicit-request DFlash baseline of ~21.31 tok/s on the real server path.

description: Attempt to run the corrected forced-length 64k stock comparison was interrupted before a usable result returned, so this stock checkpoint is invalid and should not inform decisions.

asi.hypothesis: A corrected forced-length 64k stock run is needed to tell whether the carried-up long-context DFlash shape is honestly ahead at 64k.
asi.rollback_reason: The experiment was interrupted before returning usable metrics, so the outcome is unknown and must be treated as invalid.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-run corrected 64k stock cleanly with DFlash explicitly disabled in workload env and the Qwen model-settings stanza.
asi.error_detail: run_experiment aborted before metrics were available

description: Productize fast-launch defaults/UI visibility: DFlash streaming now respects thinking_budget, streamed deltas strip leaked special tokens before chat history reuse, chat UI surfaces DFlash/fallback state and warns on reasoning-only turns, and fast launcher rotates to a fresh cache generation to avoid poisoned speculative cache state.

Retest the old authoritative-lane stabilizer `INITIAL_CACHE_BLOCKS=256` on top of the current kept fast-path/UI-visibility bundle. It stayed close to the integrated baseline but did not improve the primary metric (~106.79 vs ~106.94 current state), so the earlier cache-reservation recovery does not transfer as a win on the current tree.

Re-test explicit request-level `dflash=true` (`BENCHMARK_DFLASH=1`) on top of the current integrated fast-path/UI-authority lane. It remained essentially flat and slightly below the kept baseline (~106.79 vs ~106.94), so explicit request forcing is still not a beneficial lever on the current tree.

Re-test the broader normal-request authoritative lane with settings-driven thinking only (`ENABLE_THINKING=0`, `SETTINGS_ENABLE_THINKING=1`) on top of the current kept fast-path/UI bundle. It remained meaningfully below the integrated baseline (~105.84 vs ~106.94), so request-driven thinking is still the better current authority shape.
Mirror SD phase-1 backend (env-gated mirror_sd_mlx target split loop) wired into DFlash runtime and batched engine; authoritative 1024 Qwen server benchmark regressed badly versus bstnxbt baseline.

asi.hypothesis: A pure-MLX Mirror-SD-style prefix/suffix target split backend can improve throughput over current bstnxbt on the authoritative 1024-token lane.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric dropped to 29.09 tok/s (far below ~106.9 tok/s baseline), so candidate is not viable.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep env-gated mirror scaffolding only in tests/design; before reattempting benchmark path, align mirror runtime with bstnxbt target-cache semantics and verify split hook reuse instead of naive full verify pass.
asi.request_shape: authoritative normal-EOS thinking 1024, warmed server path

description: Mirror SD phase-1 reimplementation: mirror_sd_mlx backend now reuses bstnxbt speculative loop with Mirror-style target prefix/suffix split mode (env-gated), plus runtime/engine/backend selection wiring and tests. First honest server-path mirror baseline: 104.20 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: A low-overhead Mirror-SD target split integrated into the existing bstnxbt DFlash loop can preserve near-baseline throughput while enabling further Mirror-specific tuning.
asi.comparison_to_bstnxbt_baseline: slower than current ~106.94 authoritative keep, but establishes a working mirror backend baseline for iterative tuning

name: Mirror SD + DFlash authoritative Qwen 1024 tuning

description: Reinitialized experiment target to Mirror SD + DFlash authoritative Qwen 1024 lane and established clean mirror_sd_mlx baseline.

asi.hypothesis: Need a clean baseline under the new mirror-focused experiment config before tuning.

description: Mirror split micro-optimization: pre-resolved mirror exit layer in bstnxbt loop (avoids per-cycle env lookup) for mirror_sd_mlx path.

asi.hypothesis: Caching mirror split exit-layer resolution once per request will remove Python overhead and improve mirror_sd_mlx throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed slightly (104.26 tok/s vs 104.43 baseline).
asi.next_action_hint: Try algorithmic mirror knobs (split position and split disable A/B) instead of micro-overhead trims.

description: Mirror backend tuning: set DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_EXIT_LAYER=15 to split target forward near mid-stack instead of default capture-max split.

asi.hypothesis: A middle-layer mirror split point can reduce overhead versus near-tail splitting and improve mirror_sd_mlx throughput.

description: Mirror split tuning: DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_EXIT_LAYER=22 (deeper split point).

asi.hypothesis: A deeper mirror split near later captured layers might improve cache/mask locality for mirror_sd_mlx.
asi.rollback_reason: Catastrophic throughput collapse to 22.44 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid deep split points around 22+ on this lane; continue searching near middle/early split values around 12-18.

description: Mirror split tuning: DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_EXIT_LAYER=8 (earlier split point).

asi.hypothesis: Moving the mirror split earlier could reduce deep-prefix overhead and improve mirror path throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Major regression to 39.48 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use early split points near 8 on this workload; probe values close to 15 or test split-disable control.

description: Mirror split tuning: DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_EXIT_LAYER=14 (one layer below current best).

asi.hypothesis: A slight move from 15 to 14 could preserve mirror behavior while trimming overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Severe cliff to 46.88 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat mirror exit-layer tuning as sharply non-monotonic; keep 15 as anchor and test only very close/controlled variants (e.g., 16, split off).

description: Mirror split tuning: DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_EXIT_LAYER=16 (one layer above current best).

asi.hypothesis: Exit layer 16 may improve mirror split balance versus 15.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput dropped heavily to 67.11 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep exit layer pinned at 15; explore orthogonal mirror controls (split on/off A/B) rather than adjacent exit-layer shifts.

description: Mirror runtime A/B: added DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_SPLIT=0 control to force mirror backend through default bstnxbt target-forward mode.

asi.hypothesis: Disabling mirror split while keeping mirror backend wiring should recover or exceed mirror-split baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Regression to 63.61 tok/s on authoritative lane despite DFlash runtime path selection.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert split-disable branch; keep split-on path and focus on stable mirror split parameters or structural runtime gains.

description: Mirror backend retune: DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=14 with mirror exit layer pinned at 15.

asi.hypothesis: Mirror split path might prefer a smaller speculative block size than 15.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput dropped to 76.14 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep block tokens at 15 for mirror lane; test orthogonal knobs (cache reservation, split SDPA chunk) next.

description: Mirror backend retune: DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=16 with mirror exit layer 15.

asi.hypothesis: Mirror split path may favor a slightly larger speculative block size.
asi.rollback_reason: Throughput regressed to 69.08 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Block size is another sharp cliff in mirror lane; keep DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=15.

description: Mirror backend tuning: set INITIAL_CACHE_BLOCKS=256 on the authoritative mirror lane.

asi.hypothesis: Larger initial cache reservation may stabilize mirror backend throughput as it did on prior bstnxbt slowdowns.
asi.rollback_reason: Regression to 77.06 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not apply INITIAL_CACHE_BLOCKS uplift to mirror lane; keep cache reservation at default while tuning mirror-specific knobs.

description: Mirror lane tuning: set DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPLIT_CHUNK_SIZE=16 with mirror exit layer 15.

asi.hypothesis: Larger split-SDPA chunk size may better match mirror target split execution and improve 1024 throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Regression to 78.29 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not override split chunk size on the mirror 1024 lane; keep default chunking.

description: Mirror baseline re-run (unchanged config) to check stability.

asi.hypothesis: Unchanged mirror configuration should reproduce ~104.9 tok/s if path is stable.
asi.rollback_reason: Rerun collapsed to 80.69 tok/s without config/code changes, indicating major mirror-lane instability.
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize stability diagnostics (cache/warmup/shape effects) before further tuning; do not trust single-run mirror wins.

description: Mirror stability probe: increase warmup repeats via AR_WARMUP_REPEAT=2.

asi.hypothesis: Additional identical DFlash warmup may stabilize mirror backend and restore high-band throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Still regressed at 83.81 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Warmup repetition alone does not recover mirror instability; investigate runtime-state/cache interactions or deterministic startup isolation.

description: Mirror stability probe: isolate paged cache path with AR_CACHE_DIR=.omlx-autoresearch/cache-mirror-v2.

asi.hypothesis: Mirror instability may come from contaminated cache state; fresh cache directory should restore throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Only partial recovery to 88.65 tok/s, still below mirror baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Cache isolation alone is insufficient; inspect mirror split path semantics/acceptance rather than cache path rotation.

description: Mirror baseline re-run (unchanged config) after cache isolation probe.

asi.hypothesis: If mirror baseline is stable, rerun should return to ~104.9 tok/s.
asi.rollback_reason: Rerun remained in the ~88.8 tok/s regime, confirming sustained drift from initial mirror baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat 104.9 as likely transient; re-anchor mirror baseline if this slower regime persists and focus on deterministic runtime-path profiling.

description: Control probe: disabled mirror backend (DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=0) to measure current bstnxbt lane under same harness/settings.

asi.hypothesis: If only mirror is regressing, bstnxbt control should stay near prior 100+ band.
asi.rollback_reason: bstnxbt control itself is down to 94.30 tok/s, indicating broader regime drift beyond mirror split logic.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-anchor the current regime with fresh bstnxbt and mirror baselines, then optimize relative gap; avoid comparing directly to stale 106-era numbers.

name: Mirror SD + DFlash authoritative Qwen 1024 tuning (post-drift reanchor)

description: Reinitialized mirror experiment after regime drift and re-anchored current mirror_sd_mlx baseline (exit layer 15).

asi.hypothesis: Need a fresh baseline in the current runtime regime before further mirror tuning.

description: Control probe in re-anchored regime: disabled mirror runtime (DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=0) to measure bstnxbt baseline under same workload.

asi.hypothesis: Current mirror baseline should be compared against contemporaneous bstnxbt in the same regime.
asi.rollback_reason: Run used control (non-mirror) backend and is not a valid keep for mirror integration despite slightly higher metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Use ~96.71 tok/s as the immediate control target; tune mirror runtime above this while keeping mirror backend enabled.

description: Mirror baseline confirmation rerun (unchanged config).

asi.hypothesis: Mirror baseline should reproduce around 96 tok/s in current regime.
asi.rollback_reason: Slight regression to 95.80 tok/s versus 96.01 baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat current mirror lane as ~96 tok/s class; explore targeted knobs for incremental gain over 96.7 control.

description: Mirror split tuning: removed explicit exit-layer override to use default capture-max split.

asi.rollback_reason: Throughput regressed to 95.46 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep explicit DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_EXIT_LAYER=15; default split is weaker in current regime.

description: Mirror lane request-shape probe: BENCHMARK_DFLASH=1 (explicit request-level dflash=true).

asi.hypothesis: Explicit request-level DFlash forcing may improve mirror backend selection/perf consistency.
asi.rollback_reason: Metric regressed to 94.96 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep BENCHMARK_DFLASH=0 for mirror lane; explicit forcing is not beneficial.

description: Mirror lane request-shape probe: switch to settings-driven thinking only (ENABLE_THINKING=0, SETTINGS_ENABLE_THINKING=1).

asi.hypothesis: Settings-driven thinking may yield a better mirror request path than request-driven thinking.
asi.rollback_reason: Metric dropped to 94.45 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep request-driven thinking on mirror lane (ENABLE_THINKING=1, SETTINGS_ENABLE_THINKING=0).

description: Mirror lane retune: DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=13 (with mirror exit layer 15).

asi.hypothesis: Mirror split path may prefer slightly smaller block size than 15 in current regime.
asi.rollback_reason: Significant regression to 87.11 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep block tokens pinned at 15 for mirror lane.

description: Mirror baseline rerun (unchanged config) after retune probes.

asi.hypothesis: Unchanged mirror path may fluctuate; rerun used to estimate current noise floor.
asi.rollback_reason: No code/config change; small +0.30 tok/s uplift is likely noise, not actionable improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue code-level mirror runtime optimization for deterministic gains rather than keeping no-op rerun improvements.

description: Optimized mirror target split forward implementation: inlined prefix/suffix loops and added exit-layer resolution caching in mirror_sd_target.py. Mirror authoritative lane jumped to 105.38 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Reducing Python overhead in the mirror split target-forward helper should recover most of the mirror-vs-bstnxbt gap.

description: Mirror optimized helper confirmation rerun (unchanged config/code).

asi.hypothesis: Optimized mirror split helper should remain in the ~105 tok/s band on immediate rerun.
asi.rollback_reason: No new code/config change and metric was slightly below the previous keep (104.78 vs 105.38).
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed from commit c926eb5 and test remaining mirror knobs (exit layer micro-sweep, control comparison) for additional gains.

description: Post-optimization control probe: disabled mirror runtime (DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=0) to measure bstnxbt under same re-anchored workload.

asi.hypothesis: After mirror helper optimization, mirror backend should at least match contemporaneous bstnxbt control.
asi.rollback_reason: Control run is non-mirror backend and not a valid keep candidate; used for comparison only.

description: Mirror split micro-sweep: DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_EXIT_LAYER=16 with optimized helper implementation.

asi.hypothesis: After helper optimization, nearby split layer 16 may surpass layer 15.
asi.rollback_reason: Metric lower than mirror keep (104.38 vs 105.38).
asi.next_action_hint: Retain exit layer 15 as current optimum; probe 14/17 only if needed for map completion.

description: Mirror exit-layer=14 confirmation rerun (unchanged config/code).

description: Mirror split micro-sweep: DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_EXIT_LAYER=13.

description: Mirror optimized helper confirmation rerun (unchanged config/code) after 106.21 keep.

description: Post-optimization control rerun: disabled mirror runtime (DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=0) to measure bstnxbt under latest code.

name: Mirror SD instability diagnosis: eval-phase telemetry

description: Pivot baseline: added per-request DFlash telemetry plumbing and captured first eval-phase profile (mirror high-window sample).

asi.hypothesis: If collapses are runtime-speed driven, exposing dflash_eval_s and speculative counters on honest server runs will separate acceptance behavior from execution-speed regressions.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate repeat and control parity with telemetry enabled; test whether eval_s inflation occurs while acceptance remains flat.

description: Mirror telemetry repeat at ~8.78s eval-phase confirmed same acceptance profile and near-identical timing; tiny delta treated as noise.

asi.hypothesis: In stable high-window samples, telemetry should remain tightly clustered if instrumentation is trustworthy.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary improvement vs baseline was only ~0.005s eval_s and treated as noise-level unchanged behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Run parity control telemetry and then wait for next collapse sample to compare eval_s inflation at constant acceptance.

description: Control parity telemetry (`DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=0`) showed slightly worse eval-phase time (~8.85s) than mirror baseline (~8.79s) in this high-window pair.

asi.hypothesis: Immediate mirror/control telemetry pairing can attribute whether eval_s inflation is backend-specific or shared.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric (dflash_eval_s, lower-better) was worse than baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Restore mirror lane and wait for collapse-window sample to compare whether both backends share eval_s inflation with stable acceptance.

description: Mirror collapse-window telemetry: eval-phase time spiked to ~15.78s with throughput ~61.65 tok/s while speculative acceptance stats remained unchanged.

asi.hypothesis: Collapse events should manifest as eval_s inflation with stable draft/acceptance counters if runtime-speed is the dominant failure mode.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric (dflash_eval_s, lower-better) regressed sharply vs baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Capture additional collapse/rebound telemetry pairs and prioritize interventions targeting eval-path/runtime-state behavior rather than acceptance tuning.

description: Mirror severe collapse telemetry: eval-phase expanded to ~34.38s (throughput ~28.82 tok/s) with unchanged speculative acceptance profile.

name: Mirror SD + DDTree authoritative Qwen1024 tok/s loop

description: Re-initialized tok/s-focused segment and captured authoritative baseline after metric reset; collapse window active (safe mode on), so this serves as new tok/s baseline anchor.
DDTree wrapper path with tuned defaults (budget 12 + block-size alignment) improved tok/s in current collapse window versus tok/s baseline anchor.
Native DDTree runtime with block-size alignment (budget 12) outperformed wrapper path in the same degraded lane window, reducing tree build/verify time and lifting tok/s.
Control baseline (Mirror + BSTNXBT, no DDTree) re-entered healthy fused regime and outperformed DDTree paths in this window; updates tok/s best for the new segment.
Native DDTree retest (budget 12) stayed below the recovered Mirror+Bstnxbt control baseline in the same window; keep DDTree as non-default experimental path.
Native DDTree budget-10 probe improved over some degraded DDTree samples but remained below current Mirror+Bstnxbt tok/s baseline; keep DDTree budget 10 as non-winning on this lane.
Native DDTree: removed eager mx.eval(verify_logits) sync in tree-verify path to defer synchronization to downstream posterior/commit flow; major tok/s uplift in healthy window.
Control baseline rerun after native sync-deferral change confirms no-DDTree Mirror+Bstnxbt still leads in healthy window (~106.9 tok/s), while DDTree improved but remains below control.
Replication confirmed native DDTree sync-deferral uplift is stable (~95.4 tok/s), but it remains below the healthy Mirror+Bstnxbt control baseline (~106.9 tok/s), so DDTree stays experimental.

Added optional DDTree top-k unnormalized scoring fast-path (env-gated, default off); baseline run with flag off remained in expected native DDTree healthy band (~95 tok/s), showing no default-path regression.

asi.hypothesis: A gated top-k score path that skips full-vocab logsumexp can reduce tree-build overhead when enabled, while default-off behavior stays neutral.
asi.next_action_hint: Run matched native DDTree trial with DFLASH_DDTREE_TOPK_UNNORMALIZED=1 to measure real tok/s effect.

description: Enable unnormalized DDTree top-k scoring (skip full-vocab logsumexp) improved native DDTree healthy-window throughput (~95.0 → ~97.2 tok/s) and reduced tree-build time.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping per-step full-vocab logsumexp in DDTree top-k tree construction should cut tree-build overhead without harming acceptance/verification correctness.
asi.next_action_hint: Replicate with one more native DDTree run under DFLASH_DDTREE_TOPK_UNNORMALIZED=1, then compare against no-DDTree control in the same window.

description: Replication run for unnormalized DDTree top-k scoring stayed in the same improved band (~96.8 tok/s) but did not exceed the first sample; treat as confirmation/noise, no new code delta.

asi.hypothesis: The unnormalized top-k scoring optimization should replicate near ~97 tok/s in healthy windows.
asi.rollback_reason: No additional code change and sample was slightly below prior result.
asi.next_action_hint: Run matched no-DDTree control baseline next to measure remaining gap to lane leader.

description: Control parity check hit a severe shared collapse window (~26.5 tok/s, safe mode active, unfused-dominant); not a code regression signal for the DDTree change.

asi.hypothesis: No-DDTree control should be sampled after DDTree improvements to quantify remaining leader gap in matched windows.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed sharply due instability window and this run is diagnostic-only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run native DDTree with DFLASH_DDTREE_TOPK_UNNORMALIZED=1 immediately in this degraded window to test relative robustness vs collapsed control.

description: Immediate native-DDTree parity run in the same degraded window also collapsed (~24.9 tok/s), indicating the unnormalized top-k path does not harden against collapse-regime troughs.

asi.hypothesis: If DDTree unnormalized top-k were more robust, it might outperform collapsed control in immediate edge windows.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remained deeply regressed and did not show robustness advantage over control.
asi.next_action_hint: De-prioritize collapse-hardening claims for this tweak; continue targeting healthy-window DDTree overhead reduction while maintaining honest control parity checks.

description: Native DDTree unnormalized budget-10 probe regressed sharply in the current degraded window versus budget-12 behavior; keep budget-10 as non-winning for this lane.

asi.hypothesis: Lower DDTree budget (10) plus unnormalized top-k might reduce tree-build overhead enough to raise tok/s.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed in-window and did not show an advantage over budget-12 native behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Return to budget 12 and focus on reducing healthy-window overhead without shrinking acceptance depth.

description: Native DDTree unnormalized path with reduced fp32 casting did not show a clear uplift in this window (~64 tok/s); result is below recent improved-band samples and inconclusive under drift.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding unnecessary fp32 casting in unnormalized top-k mode could further reduce DDTree tree-build overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus recent DDTree healthy-band samples and did not provide robust evidence of improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert this variant; continue from prior kept unnormalized top-k path and prioritize matched healthy-window A/Bs.

description: Native DDTree + unnormalized top-k confirmation sample in healthy window (~97.3 tok/s); also updated/pruned autoresearch.ideas.md with current DDTree findings and stale do-not-retry entries.

asi.hypothesis: The kept unnormalized top-k path should repeatedly hit the ~97 tok/s native DDTree band in healthy windows.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with matched no-DDTree control parity checks in healthy windows and target remaining ~9–11 tok/s overhead gap.

description: Matched no-DDTree control parity in healthy window confirms lane leader remains ~107 tok/s; native DDTree (with unnormalized top-k) still trails by roughly ~9–10 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: A healthy-window control parity run is needed to quantify the remaining DDTree overhead gap after latest keeps.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus next DDTree work on reducing per-cycle overhead in healthy windows (tree build/commit path) while preserving acceptance behavior and honest parity checks.

description: Immediate DDTree re-check in the same healthy window stayed near ~97.3 tok/s, confirming consistent DDTree band but still ~9–10 tok/s below control parity.

asi.hypothesis: Back-to-back healthy-window sampling should stabilize the DDTree-vs-control gap estimate.
asi.rollback_reason: No new code delta and primary metric remains below control leader.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue DDTree overhead work (tree build + commit path) and re-run matched parity checks after each meaningful change.

description: Add env-gated DDTree top-k skip-sort path (default off) and verify default-path parity: native DDTree stayed in expected ~97 tok/s healthy band.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping in-topk sort may reduce DDTree tree-build overhead when enabled, while default-off path remains neutral.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate A/B with DFLASH_DDTREE_TOPK_SKIP_SORT=1 in the same lane window.

description: Top-k skip-sort variant (DFLASH_DDTREE_TOPK_SKIP_SORT=1) was slightly worse than sorted top-k baseline in matched healthy window; keep sorted top-k path.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping in-topk sorting would lower tree-build overhead enough to improve native DDTree tok/s.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus immediate sorted-topk baseline and build time did not improve.
asi.next_action_hint: Drop skip-sort path and continue with sorted unnormalized top-k while targeting other overheads.

description: Re-anchored native DDTree healthy-window baseline before budget sweep: ~97.45 tok/s with budget 12 and unnormalized top-k.

asi.hypothesis: Need a fresh same-window native DDTree baseline to judge budget variants without drift bias.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; baseline characterization run only.
asi.next_action_hint: Test DFLASH_DDTREE_BUDGET=14 immediately in the same lane window.

description: DDTree budget-14 probe regressed sharply (~84.8 tok/s): acceptance dropped and cycle count rose (117 cycles), with higher tree-build/eval time.

asi.hypothesis: Raising DDTree budget from 12 to 14 might increase accepted tokens per cycle and improve tok/s.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed heavily and telemetry moved in the wrong direction (lower acceptance, more cycles, higher build/eval time).
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate budget-12 back-check to separate variant effect from regime drift before updating do-not-retry guidance.

description: Immediate budget-12 back-check recovered to expected native DDTree band (~97.0 tok/s), confirming budget-14 regression was variant-driven, not regime-only drift.

asi.hypothesis: If budget-14 regression is primarily variant-driven, reverting to budget 12 should immediately restore the prior ~97 tok/s band.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; this run is an attribution confirmation sample.
asi.next_action_hint: Mark budget 14 as non-promising for this lane and test a different overhead lever (e.g., top-k branching cap below budget).

description: Top-k-cap instrumentation run landed in severe collapse window (~25.2 tok/s, eval_s ~40.6; tree_build ~8.52s), so this sample is unusable for assessing the code change.

asi.hypothesis: Adding an env-gated top-k cap (default off) should be neutral unless explicitly enabled and may allow budget-vs-branching A/Bs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric collapsed due transition/regime instability, so the run cannot validate neutrality or improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate no-DDTree control parity in the same window to confirm shared collapse attribution, then resume DDTree overhead probes after recovery.

description: Immediate no-DDTree control parity after DDTree trough also landed in degraded shared wave (~58.1 tok/s, safe mode active, mixed fused/unfused), confirming transition instability is active.

asi.hypothesis: A fresh control run on the same edge should show whether the severe DDTree trough occurred during a shared collapse regime.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic parity run only; no code changes and metric remains far below healthy-window leader.
asi.next_action_hint: Stay in passive sampling until rebound toward healthy window, then resume DDTree overhead A/B work.

description: Passive control sampling shows rebound from the trough (~69.6 tok/s) but still in degraded mixed fused/unfused regime with watchdog safe mode active.

asi.hypothesis: After fresh collapse edge, unchanged control runs should climb through staged recovery bands before returning to healthy fused-only floor.
asi.rollback_reason: No code changes; monitoring-only run remains far below healthy leader.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue passive sampling until healthy re-entry, then resume DDTree optimization A/Bs in stable windows.

description: Further control rebound reached ~78.5 tok/s with safe mode off and fused-only steps, indicating transition into a fused-only inflated shelf rather than full recovery.

asi.hypothesis: Recovery can hand off from mixed safe-mode execution into fused-only inflated shelves before returning to near-floor throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change and primary metric remains well below healthy window.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep passive sampling until near-floor (~106+) returns; avoid promoting code changes from this inflated-shelf regime.

description: Control follow-up stayed in fused-only inflated regime (~71.4 tok/s, safe mode off) rather than continuing monotonic recovery.

asi.hypothesis: If rebound is complete, fused-only control should climb toward near-floor throughput; otherwise an inflated shelf persists.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change and run remains in degraded fused-only shelf.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue passive sampling for healthy re-entry before evaluating new DDTree code variants.

description: Control resample remained in fused-only inflated shelf (~76.5 tok/s, safe mode off); still below healthy-window floor.

asi.hypothesis: Additional unchanged samples should reveal whether fused-only inflated shelf self-resolves quickly or persists.
asi.rollback_reason: No code changes; this is monitoring-only and still in degraded regime.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep passive monitoring; defer new DDTree optimization patches until window returns to near-floor behavior.

description: Native DDTree check during fused-only inflated shelf landed at ~73.0 tok/s (tree_build ~2.83s), still below contemporaneous no-DDTree control (~76.5 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: In the current inflated shelf, DDTree should still trail control if overhead gap persists independent of collapse safe-mode behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change and primary metric remains below matched control sample.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue passive window monitoring; re-run DDTree optimization A/B only after healthy-window re-entry.

description: Control lane re-entered healthy fused-only window (~106.9 tok/s, eval_s ~8.88) after inflated-shelf regime; safe mode remained inactive.

asi.hypothesis: Passive monitoring should eventually show self-recovery from fused-only inflated shelf back to healthy near-floor behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; this is regime-state monitoring rather than a candidate keep.

description: Immediate native DDTree parity in recovered healthy window remained in expected ~97.4 tok/s band, confirming persistent ~9–10 tok/s gap vs no-DDTree control.

asi.hypothesis: Once control re-enters healthy fused-only regime, native DDTree parity should return to its usual ~97 tok/s band if overhead gap remains unchanged.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change; diagnostic parity sample only, still below control lane leader.
asi.next_action_hint: Try a new env-gated overhead lever in native DDTree tree-build path with immediate healthy-window A/B.

description: DDTree budget-11 probe regressed severely in healthy window (~77.2 tok/s): acceptance collapsed (~7.88), cycle count rose (130), and tree-build time increased.

asi.hypothesis: Slightly reducing DDTree budget (12→11) might lower tree-build overhead while preserving enough acceptance to improve tok/s.

asi.next_action_hint: Add budget 11 to lane do-not-retry list and avoid sub-12 budgets on this workload.

description: Immediate budget-12 back-check restored native DDTree to normal ~97.3 tok/s band, confirming budget-11 cliff is variant-driven.

asi.hypothesis: If the budget-11 regression is causal, reverting to budget 12 should immediately recover acceptance/cycle profile and tok/s.

description: Top-k-cap instrumentation baseline (flag off) landed in a severe degraded window (~48.6 tok/s, tree_build ~4.34s), so this run cannot assess patch neutrality.

asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate no-DDTree control parity to confirm shared regime degradation, then retry instrumentation only after healthy-window recovery.

description: Immediate no-DDTree parity after DDTree trough also landed in degraded fused-only shelf (~68.8 tok/s, eval_s ~14.13), confirming shared regime drift.

asi.hypothesis: If the DDTree trough was regime-driven, immediate control parity should also show inflated eval time and depressed tok/s.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic parity run only; no code change and metric remained far below healthy window.

description: Passive control recovery climbed from trough to mid-shelf (~88.8 tok/s, fused-only, safe mode off) but still below healthy near-floor band.

asi.hypothesis: After fresh degraded parity edge, control should recover in staged fused-only bands before full near-floor return.
asi.rollback_reason: No code changes; monitoring-only sample still below healthy-window regime.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue passive monitoring until ~106+ near-floor re-entry before resuming DDTree code A/Bs.

description: Control re-entered high fused-only near-floor window (~108.5 tok/s, eval_s ~8.75), suitable for matched DDTree parity/overhead checks.

asi.hypothesis: A fresh high control sample is needed before any DDTree comparison to avoid collapse-window bias.

description: Matched native DDTree parity in the same high-window regime landed at ~98.9 tok/s with tree_build ~2.03s, keeping a ~9.6 tok/s gap vs control.

asi.hypothesis: In a stable high control window, native DDTree should reveal its true remaining overhead gap.
asi.rollback_reason: No code changes; this run is a parity measurement and remains below control lane leader.
asi.next_action_hint: Test a new DDTree runtime toggle (verify bind mode) in this stable window to probe tree-verify overhead.

description: DDTree verify bind-mode probe (DFLASH_DDTREE_NATIVE_VERIFY_BIND_MODE=all) was essentially flat/slightly worse than default target_only in matched high window.

asi.hypothesis: Binding all verify helpers to oMLX BST implementations might reduce native DDTree verify overhead and improve tok/s.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric showed no improvement versus immediate default-mode baseline; tree-build/verify timings were effectively unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not prioritize verify bind-mode=all for this lane; consider one quick model_only spot-check only if in stable high window.

description: DDTree verify bind-mode=model_only also stayed flat/slightly worse than default target_only (~98.7 tok/s), with no tree-build/verify timing benefit.

asi.hypothesis: A lighter bind set (model_only) might outperform bind-mode=all and reduce verify overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve and timing breakdown was effectively unchanged from baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Prune non-default verify bind modes (all/model_only) for this lane; keep default target_only.

description: Unexpected DDTree uplift with budget=13 (~103.4 tok/s, tree_build ~1.93s, cycles 98, avg_acceptance ~10.45) versus recent budget-12 ~99 tok/s; treat as promising but unconfirmed.

asi.hypothesis: DDTree budget 13 might improve token throughput by reducing cycle count while keeping tree-build overhead manageable.
asi.rollback_reason: No code change yet and result needs immediate replication/control parity before promoting as a lane update.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate budget-13 replication, then matched budget-12 and no-DDTree control checks in the same window to confirm robustness.

description: Budget-13 DDTree replication confirmed uplift (~103.4 tok/s) with consistent lower tree_build (~1.93s), fewer cycles (98), and higher avg_acceptance (~10.45).

asi.hypothesis: If budget-13 uplift is real, a second run should reproduce the same acceptance/cycle/tree-build profile.
asi.rollback_reason: Still env-only tuning without code change; need matched budget-12 and control checks before promoting any default update.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate budget-12 back-check in same window, then control parity to measure remaining gap.

description: Immediate budget-12 back-check stayed at ~98.8 tok/s, confirming a large matched gap versus budget-13 (~103.4 tok/s) in this window.

asi.hypothesis: If budget-13 uplift is causal, reverting to budget 12 should restore prior ~99 tok/s profile with higher tree-build and more cycles.
asi.rollback_reason: Variant under test (budget 12) underperformed matched budget-13 and this is env-only characterization.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate no-DDTree control parity, then consider promoting DDTree default budget from 12→13 via code change behind current lane assumptions.

description: Matched no-DDTree control parity remained high (~108.6 tok/s), so budget-13 DDTree narrows but does not close the control gap in this window.

asi.hypothesis: After budget-13 DDTree uplift, immediate control parity should quantify remaining gap for promotion decisions.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic control sample only; no code changes.
asi.next_action_hint: Promote a code-level default-budget change (12→13) only if neutral/stable in additional windows, and keep DDTree experimental since control still leads.

description: Promote DDTree default budget from 12→13; default-path native DDTree improved to ~103.3 tok/s with lower tree-build time and fewer cycles in matched healthy window.

asi.hypothesis: Raising default DDTree budget to 13 will reduce cycle count and total tree overhead enough to improve native DDTree throughput versus default 12.
asi.learning: Budget 13 reproducibly outperformed budget 12 in this lane window; DDTree still trails no-DDTree control but gap narrowed.

description: Post-keep control parity stayed high (~108.6 tok/s), confirming DDTree default-budget 13 narrows but does not eliminate the no-DDTree leader gap.

asi.hypothesis: After promoting DDTree default budget 13, immediate control parity should verify remaining headroom and guard against over-claiming.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic control sample only; no further code changes in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Run one post-keep DDTree confirmation sample without env budget override, then update ideas with new default and do-not-retry notes.

description: Post-keep confirmation with default budget (no env override) reproduced ~103.4 tok/s DDTree band and retained improved cycles/tree-build profile.

asi.hypothesis: If the default-budget keep is robust, repeated default-path runs should preserve budget=13 telemetry shape (cycles 98, acceptance ~10.45, tree_build ~1.93s).
asi.rollback_reason: No new code change; this is confirmation-only after keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Update autoresearch.ideas.md to reflect default-budget shift and prune stale budget-12-as-default assumption; continue matched control/DDTree monitoring.

description: DDTree budget-15 probe regressed sharply (~82.6 tok/s): acceptance dropped (~8.39), cycles increased (122), and fast-path ratio degraded (0.93).

asi.hypothesis: Increasing budget beyond 13 might further reduce cycle count and improve DDTree throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric collapsed with worse acceptance/cycle/tree-build profile, indicating over-budget cliff behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Add budget 15 to DDTree do-not-retry list and stay centered on budget 13.

description: Immediate default-budget (13) back-check returned to ~103.3 tok/s, confirming budget-15 was a variant cliff rather than regime drift.

asi.hypothesis: If budget-15 regression is causal, restoring default budget 13 should immediately recover the improved DDTree profile.
asi.rollback_reason: No new code change; this run is attribution confirmation after a bad variant.
asi.next_action_hint: Update ideas do-not-retry with budget 15 and continue budget-13 DDTree vs control monitoring.

description: Control monitoring stayed in high fused-only band (~108.7 tok/s), confirming stable no-DDTree leader after DDTree budget-13 keep.

asi.hypothesis: A fresh control sample after DDTree budget probes should verify whether high-window control behavior remains stable.
asi.rollback_reason: No code changes; monitoring-only control run.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue matched DDTree/control sampling around the new budget-13 default and avoid overfitting to a single high pocket.

description: Matched DDTree sample after high control run held stable at ~103.34 tok/s with budget=13 profile (98 cycles, ~1.92s tree-build).

asi.hypothesis: If the new default is robust, DDTree should repeatedly stay near ~103.3 tok/s with the same telemetry signature across adjacent runs.
asi.rollback_reason: No new code change; this is post-keep stability monitoring.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep budget 13 default, continue collapse-edge parity discipline, and pursue next overhead levers only when matched windows remain stable.

description: Turning off unnormalized top-k at budget 13 regressed DDTree (~99.35 tok/s): cycles rose (102), tree-build increased (~2.01s), and fast-path ratio fell (~0.81).

asi.next_action_hint: Keep unnormalized top-k enabled for DDTree experiments; do not attempt default-off DDTree comparisons as representative of best path.

description: Re-enabling unnormalized top-k immediately restored DDTree to ~103.4 tok/s and fast-path ratio 1.0, confirming prior regression was mode-specific.

asi.hypothesis: If normalized scoring caused the regression, restoring DFLASH_DDTREE_TOPK_UNNORMALIZED=1 should immediately recover the improved budget-13 profile.
asi.rollback_reason: No new code changes; this run validates mode attribution only.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep unnormalized top-k on for DDTree optimization work; focus future probes on remaining ~5 tok/s control gap.

description: Control remained in stable high fused-only regime (~108.63 tok/s), reinforcing that DDTree budget-13 sits consistently ~5 tok/s below lane leader.

asi.next_action_hint: Keep using matched control/DDTree pairs for attribution and prioritize new DDTree overhead ideas only if they can target the remaining ~5 tok/s gap.

description: Verify bind-mode=none produced essentially identical DDTree performance (~103.4 tok/s) to current default behavior; no meaningful uplift signal.

description: Add env-gated DDTree top-k cap knob (DFLASH_DDTREE_TOPK_CAP) and confirm default-off path remains in expected budget-13 ~103.4 tok/s band.

asi.hypothesis: Capping top-k below budget 13 might shave tree-build overhead without hurting acceptance enough to drop throughput.

description: Top-k cap=10 was also flat/slightly worse than uncapped budget-13 baseline; no measurable DDTree uplift.

asi.hypothesis: A stronger top-k cap might reduce tree-build work enough to improve throughput while preserving acceptance.

description: Control parity remained high (~108.67 tok/s) in fused-only stable regime, keeping DDTree budget-13 gap at roughly ~5.2 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Stable high-window control samples are needed to prevent overfitting DDTree improvements to drifting windows.

asi.hypothesis: Skipping full-vocab float32 materialization in unnormalized mode will reduce DDTree tree-build overhead on Qwen3.6 where build dominates.
asi.rollback_reason: Despite meaningful uplift, primary metric still trails baseline (~84.22 tok/s), so not promotable in this segment.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep this optimization in backlog; consider combining with additional tree-build reductions and retesting without telemetry overhead to check if it crosses baseline.

description: Qwen3.6 Mirror+DDTree budget-8 with `DFLASH_DDTREE_TOPK_SKIP_SORT=1` (telemetry off) reached ~80.00 tok/s, close to earlier best but still under no-DFlash baseline.

asi.hypothesis: Skip-sort + budget-8 might recover additional throughput without telemetry overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remains below baseline (~84.22 tok/s), so no promotion.
asi.next_action_hint: Conclude env-only tuning is plateauing below baseline; prioritize integration-path/code-level fixes for tree-build and non-Mirror divergence.

description: Qwen3.6 Mirror+DDTree skip-sort sweep point with budget=10 regressed to ~70.50 tok/s, reinforcing that useful budget range stays near ≤8 in current integration.

asi.hypothesis: Budget 10 might extend accepted depth enough to beat budget-8 plateau.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed materially versus both baseline and budget-8 runs.
asi.next_action_hint: Prune budget>8 sweeps for Qwen3.6 as stale and shift effort to structural tree-build/runtime-path fixes.

description: Qwen3.6 Mirror+DDTree budget-8 skip-sort with smaller block size (`DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=8`) regressed (~73.26 tok/s); cycles increased and fast-path ratio dropped versus block-16.

asi.hypothesis: Reducing block size might cut per-cycle tree-build cost enough to offset extra cycles in build-dominant Qwen3.6 lane.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed and telemetry showed worse acceptance dynamics (more cycles, more slow-path commits).
asi.next_action_hint: Treat block-size reductions as low-value here; keep block size at default while pursuing runtime/path-level fixes.

description: Qwen3.6 long-run no-DFlash control parity check slightly improved baseline to ~84.56 tok/s, confirming the lane is stable while DFlash/DDTree variants remain below control.

asi.hypothesis: Refreshing the control sample helps avoid over-attributing speculative regressions to transient baseline drift.

description: Rechecked best-known Qwen3.6 speculative stack (Mirror+DDTree, budget=8, unnormalized + skip-sort) and it held around ~80.05 tok/s at 1024 tokens, still below updated no-DFlash control.

asi.hypothesis: The strongest current DDTree configuration might close the gap on a fresh control window.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remains below control baseline (~84.56 tok/s).
asi.next_action_hint: Stop env-only sweeps; prioritize integration/runtime changes (tree-build hot path and non-Mirror path divergence) before further benchmark retries.

description: Re-tested code patch (skip full-vocab float32 cast in unnormalized tree-build) on best non-telemetry config (Mirror+DDTree budget-8 skip-sort): throughput improved modestly (~80.69 tok/s) but stayed below control.

asi.hypothesis: The tree-build float32-cast removal should carry over to non-telemetry runs and potentially close the remaining control gap.
asi.rollback_reason: Uplift was insufficient; primary metric still below no-DFlash baseline (~84.56 tok/s).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep this optimization as a deferred building block, but do not promote alone; need additional integration/runtime gains.

description: Pure Qwen3.6 DDTree (no Mirror) with verify bind mode `none` remained catastrophic (~16.39 tok/s): acceptance collapsed to ~1.01 tokens/cycle across ~1010 cycles, with tree-build exploding to ~24.7s.

asi.hypothesis: Disabling native verify helper bindings might resolve the non-Mirror DDTree runtime mismatch on Qwen3.6.
asi.rollback_reason: No recovery; primary metric remained drastically below baseline and acceptance collapse persisted.
asi.next_action_hint: Close non-Mirror bind-mode tuning for Qwen3.6 as stale; investigate why non-Mirror path produces near-zero acceptance compared to Mirror-assisted path.

description: Qwen3.6 Mirror+DDTree budget-8 with top-k cap (`DFLASH_DDTREE_TOPK_CAP=6`) regressed hard to ~60.25 tok/s, so capping candidate set is harmful in this lane.

asi.hypothesis: Capping DDTree top-k candidates below budget might cut tree-build overhead while preserving enough acceptance.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric collapsed versus untapped budget-8 configuration.
asi.next_action_hint: Close top-k cap tuning for Qwen3.6 lane as stale; avoid further cap sweeps.

description: Qwen3.6 plain DFlash (no Mirror/DDTree flags) remained unusable for speed target: run ended early at 17 completion tokens (`finish_reason=stop`) and measured only ~25.69 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: If we focus on raw DFlash first, plain DFlash path might provide a faster-than-baseline foundation before DDTree layering.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric far below baseline and token count mismatch (17 vs 1024) makes this configuration non-competitive for the target workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Test Mirror-only DFlash path next with fixed-length generation semantics (consider ignore_eos for apples-to-apples throughput).

description: Qwen3.6 Mirror-only DFlash (no DDTree) on full 1024-token run achieved ~66.81 tok/s, well below control baseline and below best Mirror+DDTree stack.

asi.hypothesis: Mirror-only DFlash might provide a simpler path to exceed baseline before adding DDTree complexity.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remained substantially below no-DFlash baseline (~84.56 tok/s).
asi.next_action_hint: Given plain and Mirror-only DFlash both underperform, continue with structural runtime implementation work rather than config-only tuning.

name: Qwen3.6 DFlash-first (beat no-DFlash baseline)

description: DFlash-first segment baseline: Qwen3.6 no-DFlash long-run control measured ~85.09 tok/s (1024 tokens), setting the target to beat for DFlash implementation work.

asi.hypothesis: Before DFlash-first implementation, anchor a fresh no-DFlash baseline in the new segment for strict promotion criteria.

description: DFlash-first anchor run for Mirror-only backend (no DDTree) measured ~67.02 tok/s at 1024 tokens, confirming substantial gap vs new no-DFlash baseline.

asi.hypothesis: Mirror-only DFlash is the simplest implementation target and should be the first candidate to beat control.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remains far below the DFlash-first segment baseline (~85.09 tok/s).
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to code-level DFlash runtime profiling/optimization (draft/verify/replay path), since config-only Mirror path is not competitive.

description: Mirror-only DFlash telemetry profile on Qwen3.6 confirmed major gap vs control (~66.96 vs ~85.09 tok/s); acceptance is low (~17%, mean commit ~3.58 tokens) and runtime is dominated by target eval + draft work.

asi.hypothesis: Profiling plain DFlash first will expose whether the bottleneck is acceptance collapse or verify/runtime overhead before touching DDTree.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remains far below baseline; this run is diagnostic.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus DFlash-first implementation on improving acceptance/commit density and reducing draft overhead in mirror path (block policy, drafter quality/conditioning, cache replay costs).

description: DFlash-first Mirror-only telemetry sweep with `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=8` improved throughput to ~71.77 tok/s and raised acceptance (~30%), but still trails no-DFlash control.

asi.hypothesis: Smaller mirror-only block size can increase acceptance/commit density enough to recover significant DFlash throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric still below baseline (~85.09 tok/s) despite improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue block-size sweep around 10–12 to seek better acceptance/time tradeoff before code changes.

description: DFlash-first Mirror-only telemetry sweep with `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=12` reached ~71.12 tok/s, slightly below block-8 result; larger block lowered acceptance and did not improve total throughput.

asi.hypothesis: Block size 12 might balance draft overhead and acceptance better than block 8 in mirror-only DFlash.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remains below baseline and underperformed the block-8 candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep block-size focus near 8 or lower; next try block 6 or adaptive policy if available.

description: DFlash-first Mirror-only telemetry sweep with `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=6` produced the best DFlash-only result so far (~81.98 tok/s), driven by much higher acceptance (~43%) and lower eval time, but still below control baseline.

asi.hypothesis: Further reducing block size can substantially raise acceptance density and close the DFlash-only gap to control.
asi.rollback_reason: Despite strong gain, metric still trails baseline (~85.09 tok/s), so not yet promotable.
asi.next_action_hint: Probe nearby block sizes (5 and 7) and potentially sink/window adjustments to push DFlash-only above baseline.

description: DFlash-first Mirror-only telemetry sweep with `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=5` collapsed to ~45.52 tok/s; watchdog safe mode activated and eval-step spikes appeared, making this block size unstable.

asi.hypothesis: Pushing block size lower than 6 might further improve acceptance and close the remaining gap to control.
asi.rollback_reason: Run regressed catastrophically with collapse-safe-mode activation.
asi.next_action_hint: Close block-size<6 sweeps as unstable for Qwen3.6 mirror-only path; keep focus at block=6 and nearby structural runtime changes.

description: Mirror-only DFlash block=6 recheck without telemetry unexpectedly collapsed to ~46.15 tok/s, indicating severe run-to-run instability/collapse behavior in this configuration.

asi.hypothesis: Removing telemetry overhead from the promising block=6 setup might further improve throughput toward baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Observed catastrophic regression; likely instability wave rather than robust configuration behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Immediately rerun identical block=6 config to distinguish transient collapse from stable performance.

description: Repeat of mirror-only DFlash block=6 without telemetry remained severely regressed (~55.16 tok/s), confirming non-telemetry block-6 behavior is unstable/non-robust despite earlier strong telemetry sample.

asi.hypothesis: If previous collapse was transient noise, an immediate rerun of block=6 without telemetry should recover near prior ~82 tok/s telemetry level.
asi.rollback_reason: Second run still far below baseline, so this configuration is not robustly performant.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat block-6 gains as instrumentation-sensitive/unstable; run no-DFlash control parity and avoid claiming block-6 as viable.

description: No-DFlash control parity check dropped sharply to ~71.42 tok/s, indicating a global instability/collapse wave; recent DFlash-first A/B conclusions are unreliable until control floor recovers.

asi.hypothesis: Control rerun should stay near ~85 tok/s if system state is stable and DFlash regressions are lane-local.
asi.rollback_reason: Control itself regressed heavily, invalidating near-term comparative attribution.
asi.next_action_hint: Pause optimization claims, monitor control until baseline band recovers, then resume DFlash-first probes.

description: Second no-DFlash control monitor partially recovered to ~75.04 tok/s but remains far below the ~85 baseline, so instability wave is still active.

description: Control-only monitor (no DFlash) continued recovering to ~79.18 tok/s but remains below the ~85 baseline band, so attribution window is still degraded.

description: Mirror-only DFlash telemetry run with `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=7` plus `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=48` reached ~83.85 tok/s, improving over prior block-7 telemetry and narrowing the gap to control.

name: Qwen3.6 DFlash-first natural-EOS robust prompt parity

description: Benchmark-honesty fix: control path now sends explicit `dflash=false` and disables model-level dflash settings when `ENABLE_DFLASH=0`; rebaselined no-DFlash Qwen3.6 at ~85.80 tok/s (full 1024 tokens).

asi.hypothesis: Explicitly forcing dflash off in control requests will eliminate inherited model-setting contamination and restore trustworthy parity baselines.

diagnostic_findings.harness_fix: explicit dflash false payload + disable dflash_enabled in autoresearch.sh when ENABLE_DFLASH=0

description: With honest control semantics in place, mirror-only DFlash (`BLOCK_TOKENS=7`, `DRAFT_SINK=48`) reached ~87.30 tok/s, clearly surpassing the corrected no-DFlash baseline (~85.80).

description: Repeat of mirror-only DFlash winner (`BLOCK_TOKENS=7`, `DRAFT_SINK=48`) stayed above the corrected no-DFlash baseline with ~87.00 tok/s on full 1024-token run.

description: Immediate no-DFlash parity check (with explicit `dflash=false` semantics) landed at ~84.93 tok/s, below segment baseline and below the DFlash winner, supporting continued DFlash advantage in this window.

description: `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=40` on mirror-only block=7 catastrophically regressed to ~45.19 tok/s (full 1024 tokens), indicating this sink is below a stability threshold for Qwen3.6 in current runtime.

description: `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=56` also collapsed mirror-only block=7 throughput to ~50.72 tok/s, confirming a narrow stable region around sink=48 on Qwen3.6.

description: Implemented Qwen3.6 porting micro-optimization from dflash-mlx verify flow: compute acceptance first, slice `committed_hidden` to `commit_count` before eval/commit, and reuse sliced tensor directly as next `target_hidden`; first run improved to ~86.48 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Evaluating only committed hidden timesteps (instead of full verify block) should reduce per-step eval pressure and improve packed-QKV throughput.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart, then a repeat DFlash sample to validate same-window separation and repeatability.

changes: Moved acceptance_length/commit_count computation before hidden-state eval.
Sliced extracted committed_hidden to [:,:commit_count,:] prior to mx.eval.
Reused sliced committed_hidden directly for target_hidden (removed post-eval slice).

description: Ported dflash-mlx verify-block plumbing into oMLX qwen3.6 runtime: added verify-length cap (`DFLASH_VERIFY_LEN`) and optional chunked verify forward (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS`) with dict-hidden concat, and wired commit logic to verified token span; default run stayed above baseline at ~86.05 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Replicating upstream verify-block controls will reduce verification-path variance and unlock larger tuning steps without regressing default behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart, then test a concrete chunk/cap variant in the same window.

changes: Added _resolve_verify_len_cap and _resolve_verify_chunk_tokens env resolvers.
Added _verify_target_block with optional chunked forward and hidden-state dict concatenation.
Updated generation loop to verify over verify_token_ids span and compute acceptance/commit from verified span.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart measured ~86.11 tok/s, essentially tying/slightly exceeding the 86.05 DFlash sample after verify-block port (parity noise, no clear edge).

asi.next_action_hint: Use the new verify knobs for larger-effect probes (e.g., verify chunk/cap variants) instead of over-sampling parity noise.

description: Tested new verify-length cap knob with `DFLASH_VERIFY_LEN=6` (block=7); throughput collapsed to ~74.68 tok/s, indicating reduced verify span hurts commit efficiency more than it saves verify compute.

asi.hypothesis: Slightly capping verified tokens per cycle might reduce verify cost enough to improve throughput in qwen3.6.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed sharply below baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep verify_len uncapped (default=block tokens); if revisiting, only test in much more stable windows with careful acceptance telemetry.

description: Probe with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=4` crashed the benchmark request (`requests.exceptions.ChunkedEncodingError: Response ended prematurely`), indicating the new chunked verify path is not robust yet.

asi.hypothesis: Chunking verify forward passes might smooth latency and improve stability without hurting acceptance.
asi.rollback_reason: Runtime crashed during benchmark request with chunked verify enabled.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat verify-chunk mode as unstable; keep chunking disabled by default and capture server traceback before any retry.

description: Hardened verify-chunk port for qwen3.6 mirror path by auto-disabling `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS` outside default target-forward mode (prevents mirror chunking crash); default run remained above baseline at ~85.89 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Chunked verify is not currently safe with mirror target-forward override, so gating it off in that mode should preserve stability without hurting default throughput.
asi.next_action_hint: Record verify-chunk-on-mirror as stale/unsafe in ideas; continue with stable default path and seek larger-effect runtime improvements.

changes: Added guard to ignore verify chunk tokens when target_forward_mode != default (mirror path).

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart measured ~86.06 tok/s, slightly above the 85.89 DFlash sample after verify-chunk hardening (near-parity, no clear DFlash edge).

asi.hypothesis: Same-window control would show whether the hardening patch changed parity or mainly improved runtime safety.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; parity delta is noise-scale.
asi.next_action_hint: Focus next on code paths with larger expected effect than sub-0.2 tok/s parity movement.

description: Post-hardening DFlash rerun reached ~86.60 tok/s, confirming the qwen3.6 hook-layering + verify-block port path still sits in the high-86 pocket.

asi.hypothesis: If recent runtime hardening is non-regressive, DFlash should continue reproducing high-86 runs in recovered windows.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart to measure same-window parity spread.

description: Major qwen3.6 mirror-lane port step: added env-gated option to bypass mirror target-forward override and route mirror runtime through the shared default bstnxbt target-forward path (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_MIRROR_USE_DEFAULT_TARGET_FORWARD=1`); first run improved to ~86.96 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: The custom mirror target-forward override is likely unnecessary overhead/fragility in qwen3.6; reusing the shared default target-forward path should improve throughput and reduce mode-specific instability.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart, then re-run DFlash with this flag in the same window to check separation beyond noise.

changes: Added `_mirror_use_default_target_forward()` env resolver.
Mirror iterate/execute now switch between `target_forward_mode=default` (no override) and legacy mirror override path via env flag.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after default-forward mirror port measured ~86.04 tok/s; paired gap vs DFlash sample is +0.92 tok/s (directionally positive for DFlash, but still single-window/noise-sensitive).

asi.hypothesis: A same-window explicit control would indicate whether the default-forward mirror port created real DFlash separation versus parity drift.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; keep/discard decision remains on DFlash candidate run.
asi.next_action_hint: Run one more DFlash repeat with the new flag to test if the +~0.9 tok/s edge persists before promoting as robust.

description: Repeat DFlash run with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_MIRROR_USE_DEFAULT_TARGET_FORWARD=1` stayed above baseline at ~86.24 tok/s, confirming the new major port remains in the high-86 shelf after the first +0.92 control gap sample.

asi.hypothesis: If the mirror-default-forward port is real, repeated DFlash samples should stay above baseline and not collapse immediately after control checks.
asi.next_action_hint: Run another explicit no-DFlash counterpart to see whether separation persists or compresses to parity noise.

description: Second explicit no-DFlash counterpart hit ~87.02 tok/s, overtaking recent DFlash samples (86.96/86.24) and collapsing the apparent edge from the mirror-default-forward port back to parity/noise.

asi.hypothesis: A second control in the same recovery pocket would test whether the initial +0.92 DFlash gap was robust.
asi.rollback_reason: Control sample outperformed DFlash; no stable separation to attribute to this code path yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid over-claiming this port as a throughput win; keep it as a structural simplification candidate and probe for larger-effect changes.

description: Retested `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=4` with mirror runtime routed to default target-forward path (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_MIRROR_USE_DEFAULT_TARGET_FORWARD=1`), but benchmark still crashed with `ChunkedEncodingError: Response ended prematurely`.

asi.hypothesis: Default target-forward routing might make verify chunking safe/usable in the qwen3.6 mirror lane.

asi.next_action_hint: Treat verify chunking as globally unsafe for now; keep it disabled and prioritize other large runtime ports.

description: Post-crash baseline DFlash rerun collapsed to ~57.42 tok/s (request ~17.83s), indicating a fresh global regime downshift rather than a stable comparison window.

description: Explicit no-DFlash control also collapsed (~71.92 tok/s), confirming a global instability wave after the chunking crash attempt; this window is not suitable for fine-grained parity attribution.

asi.hypothesis: The latest low-70/60 shelf was a transient regime event and may rebound without code changes.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart in the recovered window surged to ~89.92 tok/s, clearly above the paired DFlash rebound (~87.09); current mirror-default-forward stack still lacks robust DFlash separation.
