description: Implemented major adaptive block-token controller scaffold in qwen3.6 bstnxbt runtime (windowed acceptance-based shrink/grow, cooldown, safe-mode clamping) and switched per-step token staging to a reusable block buffer; default behavior remains off (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_ADAPTIVE_BLOCK_TOKENS=0`) and baseline run improved to ~87.94 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: A robust, bidirectional adaptive block controller plus reduced per-step buffer allocation can provide a larger next-step optimization path while preserving default behavior when disabled.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate no-DFlash counterpart, then test adaptive mode ON with conservative thresholds in the same window.

changes: Added `_AdaptiveBlockConfig`/`_AdaptiveBlockState` and env resolvers for adaptive block control.
Added `_update_adaptive_block_tokens()` with warmup/window/cooldown and bidirectional grow/shrink logic.
Reused a preallocated `block_token_buffer` in generation loop instead of allocating a fresh block tensor each step.
Added adaptive telemetry fields (current/min/max tokens, window acceptance mean, grow/shrink/clamp events).

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after adaptive-controller scaffold landed measured ~89.56 tok/s, exceeding paired DFlash default-off sample (~87.94); same-window throughput edge remains control-favored.

asi.hypothesis: Same-window control would test whether the new scaffold changed DFlash separation or just tracked regime movement.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; no robust DFlash edge in this window.
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to direct adaptive-on probe, but treat gains as provisional unless control no longer dominates in matched pairs.

description: Enabled new adaptive block controller (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_ADAPTIVE_BLOCK_TOKENS=1`, min=6 max=7) for qwen3.6 mirror-default-forward lane; throughput regressed to ~83.36 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Windowed bidirectional adaptive block sizing might stabilize low-acceptance cycles and improve throughput versus fixed block=7.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed below baseline in immediate trial.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep adaptive controller code landed but leave it disabled by default; retest only with telemetry to confirm whether block-size adjustments are over-triggering in this lane.

description: Post-revert default DFlash rerun (adaptive controller disabled) recovered to ~87.68 tok/s, indicating the regression was tied to adaptive-on settings rather than a sustained global collapse.

asi.hypothesis: If adaptive-on regression was configuration-specific, reverting to default fixed block behavior should recover prior high-87 performance.
asi.next_action_hint: Run explicit no-DFlash counterpart to verify whether high-87 DFlash is truly separated or still within parity noise.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after the default-off adaptive scaffold rerun measured ~89.21 tok/s, again overtaking paired DFlash (~87.68); current window remains control-favored.

asi.hypothesis: A matched control after the recovered DFlash run would show whether the high-87 sample represented real DFlash edge.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; no robust DFlash separation.
asi.next_action_hint: Pause parity claims; prune adaptive-on path as currently regressive and prioritize other structural runtime ports.

description: Major routing probe: disabled mirror runtime wrapper (`DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=0`) while keeping bstnxbt runtime stack active; run reached ~87.77 tok/s, showing non-mirror bstnxbt path remains viable in qwen3.6 with current ports.

asi.hypothesis: Now that mirror runtime can route through default forward, removing mirror wrapper entirely may reduce overhead or simplify the path without losing performance.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart, then compare against mirror-on DFlash in same window to determine if this is real separation or parity noise.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after non-mirror bstnxbt probe hit ~89.65 tok/s, clearly above paired DFlash ~87.77; this routing change did not establish DFlash edge in-window.

asi.hypothesis: A matched control after disabling mirror runtime wrapper would reveal whether apparent DFlash gain was real or just window movement.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; control remains faster in same window.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep routing simplification as optional but avoid promoting it as throughput win; continue searching for larger DFlash-path compute reductions.

description: Repeat DFlash run on non-mirror bstnxbt routing (`DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=0`) improved to ~88.40 tok/s, confirming this path can consistently operate in the high-88 range.

asi.hypothesis: If non-mirror bstnxbt routing is stable, repeated DFlash samples should stay in the high-87/88 band rather than collapsing.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart again to verify whether high-88 closes the current control gap.

description: Second explicit no-DFlash counterpart in the non-mirror bstnxbt window hit ~89.92 tok/s, still above paired DFlash high-88 sample (~88.40), so control remains ahead despite DFlash recovery.

asi.hypothesis: A second matched control would show whether the high-88 non-mirror DFlash run had closed the control gap.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; control remains faster in repeated same-window checks.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat non-mirror routing as an operationally viable alternative, but not a demonstrated throughput win yet.

description: Optimized non-stream execute path by avoiding per-commit full output-token list copies (`iterate_bstnxbt_mlx_generate_commits(copy_output_token_ids=False)` when no callback); first run measured ~87.64 tok/s on current non-mirror bstnxbt lane.

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating repeated full-list copies in execute mode should reduce Python overhead in DFlash commit loop for non-stream benchmark requests.
asi.next_action_hint: Run explicit no-DFlash control counterpart, then repeat DFlash to check whether any edge survives against current control-high window.

changes: Added `copy_output_token_ids` parameter to iterate function (default true).
Execute path now passes `copy_output_token_ids=False` when no on_commit callback, avoiding per-commit output list copies.
Kept streaming/callback behavior unchanged via default copy path.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after execute-copy-elision patch measured ~89.50 tok/s, staying well above paired DFlash sample (~87.64); control remains faster in this window.

asi.hypothesis: Matched control after removing per-commit output-id copies would show whether reduced Python overhead creates real DFlash separation.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; no sustained DFlash edge.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat this patch as low-risk structural cleanup but not a proven throughput gain; continue targeting larger compute-path reductions.

description: A/B probe turning off Qwen3Next packing hooks (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_PACK_TARGET=0`, `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_PACK_ATTENTION=0`) produced ~87.71 tok/s—near prior packed runs and below the recent 88.40 peak, so no robust improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Packed-QKV path might now be neutral or harmful in the non-mirror runtime window, so disabling it could improve throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: No clear primary gain over recent packed configuration; result is noise-level/parity.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep packed path as default for now; prioritize larger compute reductions rather than pack on/off toggles.

description: Routing recheck with mirror runtime re-enabled (`DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=1`) returned ~87.66 tok/s, effectively matching recent non-mirror samples and below the high-88 peak; no robust routing winner.

asi.hypothesis: Re-enabling mirror runtime might recover throughput versus the non-mirror variant.
asi.rollback_reason: No clear gain; routing result is parity/noise-level.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat mirror on/off as equivalent in current window and stop spending budget on this toggle unless behavior diverges in future regressions.

description: Added no-yield fast execute path for non-stream generation: `execute_bstnxbt_mlx_generate()` now runs `iterate_bstnxbt_mlx_generate_commits(emit_commits=False)` and consumes final tokens from StopIteration value, avoiding per-commit generator handoff overhead when no callback; first run measured ~87.70 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Removing per-commit generator yields in non-stream execute mode should cut Python overhead beyond copy-elision alone.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart, then a second DFlash sample to verify whether this structural execute-path change produces durable separation.

changes: Added `emit_commits` parameter to iterate function (default true for streaming/callback behavior).
Iterate now returns final token list as generator return value.
Execute path now uses non-yield mode when `on_commit` is absent and captures final tokens from StopIteration.value.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after no-yield execute-path optimization measured ~89.44 tok/s, still above paired DFlash sample (~87.70); control remains faster in-window.

asi.hypothesis: A matched control run would show whether the new non-yield execute path created measurable DFlash separation.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; no sustained DFlash edge.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep this as structural runtime cleanup but continue hunting higher-impact target/draft compute reductions for real separation.

description: Micro-optimization to skip per-step forced-prefix list construction when thinking is disabled yielded ~87.64 tok/s, effectively flat/slightly worse vs immediate prior no-yield runtime sample (~87.70).

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding pointless forced-prefix list work in the common no-thinking path would trim Python overhead in the commit loop.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was flat/slightly worse; no meaningful gain.
asi.next_action_hint: Drop this micro-path and focus on larger target/draft compute reductions rather than Python branch cleanups.

description: Post-revert DFlash monitor on current non-mirror/no-yield stack came in at ~87.38 tok/s, below recent high-87/88 samples; no new improvement signal.

asi.hypothesis: Current stack might sustain the prior high-87/88 shelf after reverting the no-thinking micro-opt.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric drifted down versus recent samples; monitor run only.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep focusing on larger compute-path changes; avoid promoting based on single sub-88 samples.

description: Implemented execute-mode device-buffer fast path in bstnxbt loop: when `emit_commits=0` and no thinking, commit tokens are written into a preallocated MX buffer and converted to Python list once at end, avoiding per-step token host copies/stop scans. First run measured ~87.78 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding per-step commit token host conversion in non-stream execute mode will reduce Python overhead beyond no-yield optimization.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart, then rerun DFlash to verify whether this path improves relative separation or only tracks control drift.

changes: Added execute-only device output buffer (`generated_token_buffer`) path when `emit_commits=False` and thinking is disabled.
Switched loop accounting to `generated_token_count` for buffered mode.
Added MX-based stop-token detection for buffered mode and single final host conversion of output tokens.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after execute-device-buffer optimization measured ~89.42 tok/s, above paired DFlash sample (~87.78); same-window control remains faster.

asi.hypothesis: Same-window control run would determine whether execute-device-buffer path improved real DFlash separation or only absolute DFlash speed.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; no DFlash edge in this window.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep this as structural cleanup but prioritize changes that reduce target/draft compute rather than host-overhead micro-optimizations.

description: Tested sliced-per-layer context concat for commit-hidden extraction (`extract_context_feature_from_dict_sliced`) so slice-before-eval avoids concatenating full verify span before token slicing; result ~87.62 tok/s was flat/slightly worse than nearby runs, no robust gain.

asi.hypothesis: Slicing each captured layer before concat should reduce commit-hidden assembly overhead when acceptance commits fewer tokens than verify span.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric showed no meaningful uplift versus nearby DFlash samples.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep existing commit-hidden path; prioritize larger target-forward compute reductions over concat micro-optimizations.

description: Added env-controlled exact-small-proj pad setting for linear speculative hooks (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_EXACT_SMALL_PROJ_PAD_M`, default 16) so qwen3.6 lane can A/B pad strategy without code edits; default run stayed in high-87 (~87.54 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: The fixed pad_m=16 choice may be suboptimal for block-7 qwen3.6; exposing pad_m as an env knob enables larger-effect kernel-shape tuning.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate pad_m variant (e.g., 8) in same window, then explicit no-DFlash control if variant appears promising.

changes: Iterate path now resolves `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_EXACT_SMALL_PROJ_PAD_M` and passes it to `install_target_speculative_hooks(...)`.

description: A/B probe with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_EXACT_SMALL_PROJ_PAD_M=8` produced ~87.70 tok/s vs nearby default-pad sample ~87.54; uplift is small/noise-scale and not robust enough to promote as lane default.

asi.hypothesis: Reducing exact-small-proj pad target from 16 to 8 for block-7 workload might cut unnecessary padded linear work and improve throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Gain is too small/noise-limited for default promotion.
asi.next_action_hint: Try one more distinct pad value (e.g., 12) before deciding whether this knob is worth further budget.

description: Second pad-size A/B probe with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_EXACT_SMALL_PROJ_PAD_M=12` landed at ~87.58 tok/s, essentially parity with default (16) and below the pad=8 sample; no robust winner.

asi.hypothesis: An intermediate pad target (12) might balance kernel shape efficiency better than 16 in block-7 qwen3.6 lane.
asi.rollback_reason: Result is parity/noise-level with no clear directional advantage.
asi.next_action_hint: Close exact-small-proj pad sweeps for now; move budget back to larger runtime-path changes.

description: Hardened rollback tape recording for potential verify-chunk paths: `RecurrentRollbackCache.record_tape()` now appends per-chunk tape/k/g/qkv slices while armed instead of overwriting, preserving full verified span for rollback replay. Default (non-chunked) run remained stable at ~87.71 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Chunked verify likely crashes because recurrent rollback tape is overwritten per chunk; accumulating chunk tapes should preserve correctness/state and prevent crash conditions.
asi.next_action_hint: Retest `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=4` with this tape-append fix in the same lane to check if crash is resolved.

changes: `record_tape()` now concatenates chunk slices along seq axis when `_armed` and previous tape exists.
Maintains previous behavior for single-call/non-chunk cases.

description: Retested verify chunking (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=4`) after tape-append rollback fix; benchmark still crashed with `ChunkedEncodingError: Response ended prematurely` (server terminated).

asi.hypothesis: Accumulating recurrent rollback tape across verify chunks would resolve chunking crash by preserving full replay state.
asi.rollback_reason: Runtime still crashes with verify chunking enabled; tape accumulation alone is insufficient.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep verify chunking disabled; investigate crash via lower-level server traceback before any further chunking retries.

description: Post-crash recovery DFlash check (chunking disabled) returned ~87.58 tok/s, indicating lane recovered to normal high-87 shelf but no new improvement signal.

asi.hypothesis: After chunking crash/revert, baseline DFlash path should recover rather than collapse.
asi.rollback_reason: Recovery monitor only; no new optimization effect.
asi.next_action_hint: Run explicit no-DFlash counterpart to confirm whether control still leads in current shelf.

description: Explicit no-DFlash counterpart after post-crash DFlash recovery measured ~89.17 tok/s, again above paired DFlash (~87.58); control remains faster in this window.

asi.hypothesis: Same-window control check after chunking-recovery run would verify whether DFlash recovered edge or just absolute throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; control still leads.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep chunking disabled and continue focusing on larger DFlash compute-path changes that can exceed current control-high shelf.

description: Ported upstream cache policy for verify-chunk mode: `make_target_cache()` now supports `enable_speculative_linear_cache` and uses `ArraysCache` for linear layers when chunked verify is enabled; iterate path now resolves chunking before cache creation and selects speculative rollback cache only when chunking is off. Default non-chunk run remained strong at ~87.78 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Verify chunk crashes may stem from using rollback linear caches in chunked mode; upstream disables speculative linear cache for chunking, so matching that policy should improve robustness.
asi.next_action_hint: Immediately retry `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=4` with this cache-policy port to test if crash resolves.

changes: `make_target_cache()` now accepts `enable_speculative_linear_cache` and selects `ArraysCache(size=2)` for linear layers when false.
Iterate function resolves verify chunk mode before cache construction.
Added telemetry field `speculative_linear_cache` for attribution.

description: Breakthrough probe: enabling verify chunking (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=4`) after upstream cache-policy port (chunking -> `ArraysCache` linear caches) no longer crashes and produced a massive throughput jump to ~142.76 tok/s (request ~7.17s).

asi.hypothesis: Chunking needed non-speculative linear caches (upstream policy) to be stable; once aligned, chunked verify could materially improve throughput.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control, then perform a DFlash repeat and basic output-sanity comparison to ensure this is a real optimization and not a correctness artifact.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart measured ~89.71 tok/s, far below the new chunked-DFlash run (~142.76); same-window gap is +~53.05 tok/s for DFlash.

asi.hypothesis: A matched control run would determine whether the 142 tok/s result was global regime movement or a true DFlash-path effect.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; used to classify separation.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate DFlash repeat with chunking to confirm the +50+ tok/s separation reproduces before any promotion claims.

description: Reproduction run for chunked-DFlash path (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=4`) held at ~142.55 tok/s, confirming the prior 142.76 result and sustained +~52.84 tok/s separation vs matched control.

asi.hypothesis: If chunked-DFlash improvement is real, an immediate repeat should stay in the same high-140 band.
asi.next_action_hint: Run one additional DFlash sample and then perform a lightweight output sanity check (token/text diff vs no-DFlash) to guard against correctness drift.

description: A/B on verify-chunk knob with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=8` (>= block size 7, so effectively no split while still selecting non-speculative linear cache policy) improved further to ~148.85 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: The major gain is likely from linear cache policy (`ArraysCache`/native linear path) rather than chunk splitting itself; using chunk size >= block should preserve gain without split overhead.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control, then DFlash repeat at `VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=8` to confirm sustained separation.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after `VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=8` run measured ~89.31 tok/s, leaving a very large same-window DFlash gap (+~59.53 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: A same-window control run would confirm whether the 148 tok/s DFlash result is a true DFlash-path shift rather than global regime lift.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; not an optimization candidate by itself.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate DFlash repeat at `VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=8` and then sanity-check output quality/consistency versus control.

description: DFlash repeat with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=8` reproduced at ~148.87 tok/s (request ~6.88s), confirming sustained high-throughput behavior after same-window control check.

asi.hypothesis: If the `VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=8` path is stable, repeated runs should remain in the high-140 band.
asi.next_action_hint: Run one explicit control text sanity comparison and then parameterize this cache-policy switch as a dedicated env toggle (separate from chunk-size semantics).

description: Added explicit cache-policy override knob `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE` (auto/0/1) and wired iterate path to resolve linear-cache policy via this knob; with existing `VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS=8` config, throughput stayed at ~148.84 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Decoupling linear-cache policy from verify-chunk size will enable cleaner A/B isolation (cache policy vs chunking) and safer tuning.
asi.next_action_hint: Test `VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS` unset + `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0` to verify whether chunking itself is unnecessary for the high-throughput mode.

changes: Added `_resolve_speculative_linear_cache_enabled()` env resolver.
`use_speculative_linear_cache` now uses the new override (default auto keeps prior behavior).

description: Decoupling test succeeded: with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_VERIFY_CHUNK_TOKENS` unset and `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`, throughput stayed at ~148.84 tok/s. This shows the high-throughput mode comes from non-speculative linear cache policy, not verify chunk splitting.

asi.hypothesis: The speedup is driven by linear-cache policy switch (RecurrentRollbackCache -> ArraysCache/native linear call), not by chunked verify splitting.
asi.next_action_hint: Run explicit no-DFlash control counterpart, then test `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1` for direct A/B confirmation of policy impact.

description: Explicit no-DFlash counterpart for the decoupled cache-policy run measured ~89.70 tok/s, maintaining a large same-window DFlash gap (+~59.15 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: Control check would verify whether decoupled `SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0` gain is truly DFlash-specific.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; no optimization effect by itself.
asi.next_action_hint: Run direct DFlash A/B with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1` to confirm policy toggle is the causal lever.

description: Direct A/B confirmation: forcing `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1` dropped throughput back to ~87.56 tok/s from ~148.84 with value `0`, proving the cache-policy switch is the dominant lever.

asi.hypothesis: If cache-policy override is the true cause, setting speculative linear cache back on should collapse the high-140 performance mode.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed massively versus current best configuration.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0` as active high-throughput mode and proceed with robustness/correctness checks before broader promotion.

description: Reverted to `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0` and reproduced high-throughput mode at ~148.75 tok/s, confirming the toggle is stable and reversible.

asi.hypothesis: If the cache-policy lever is robust, switching back to non-speculative linear cache should immediately restore high-140 throughput.
asi.next_action_hint: Add this new high-throughput mode and closed toggles to ideas backlog, then begin correctness/quality guard checks before wider promotion.

description: Explicit no-DFlash control sanity run measured ~89.53 tok/s; control text preview remained coherent (finish_reason=length), while prior high-throughput DFlash run also produced coherent markdown-style preview, so no obvious immediate text-quality collapse from the new mode.

asi.hypothesis: If the high-throughput mode is a correctness artifact, output previews might degrade versus control despite speed gains.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution/sanity sample only; not an optimization result.
asi.next_action_hint: Return to DFlash mode and continue validating with repeated runs plus targeted quality checks beyond preview text.

diagnostic_findings.prior_dflash_text_preview_head: # Speculative Decoding: A detailed explanation of speculative decoding, draft, verification...

description: High-throughput mode (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`) reproduced again at ~148.95 tok/s (new local high in this lane), with coherent preview output and stable request time (~6.87s).

asi.hypothesis: If the new mode is stable, repeated DFlash runs should remain near ~149 tok/s with consistent request timing.
asi.next_action_hint: Record this as active winning shape in ideas and prioritize deeper correctness verification before additional perf micro-tuning.

description: Retested block size under new high-throughput cache-policy mode (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`): increasing `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS` from 7 to 8 improved further to ~150.83 tok/s (new best in this lane).

asi.hypothesis: Previous block=8 cliff was tied to older speculative-rollback path; in the new non-spec linear cache mode, larger block may now be beneficial.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control, then DFlash repeat at block=8 to confirm separation and stability.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after block=8 high-throughput DFlash run measured ~89.40 tok/s, preserving a very large same-window DFlash gap (+~61.43 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: Control check would verify whether block=8 gain under non-spec linear cache remains DFlash-specific.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only; no optimization effect.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at block=8 to confirm stability before exploring further block/sink retunes in this new mode.

description: DFlash repeat at `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=8` + `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0` reproduced at ~150.82 tok/s, confirming the new high-throughput best mode is stable.

asi.hypothesis: If block=8 is a real improvement in the new cache-policy regime, immediate rerun should stay near ~151 tok/s.
asi.next_action_hint: Update ideas with new dominant config and invalidate old block=8 stale warning for this mode; then cautiously probe one adjacent block value (e.g., 9) in matched checks.

description: Adjacent block sweep in new mode: increasing `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS` to 9 (with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`) jumped throughput to ~176.89 tok/s (request ~5.79s), a major new best.

asi.hypothesis: With non-spec linear cache mode active, larger speculative blocks may now scale throughput strongly instead of cliffing as in the old regime.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control, then DFlash repeat at block=9 before exploring even larger blocks.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after block=9 DFlash best measured ~89.07 tok/s, preserving an even larger same-window DFlash gap (+~87.82 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: Control parity check would verify whether the block=9 jump is truly DFlash-driven in the same window.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at block=9 to verify stability, then test one adjacent block (10) cautiously.

description: DFlash repeat at block=9 + non-spec linear cache mode held very high at ~176.42 tok/s, confirming the new high-170 shelf is stable after control check.

asi.hypothesis: If block=9 mode is robust, repeat should stay near the initial ~176.9 result.
asi.next_action_hint: Probe one adjacent block value (`DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=10`) to map whether scaling continues or cliffs.

description: Adjacent block sweep continued upward in new mode: `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=10` (with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`) improved to ~184.45 tok/s (request ~5.55s), setting another best.

asi.hypothesis: The non-spec linear cache regime may favor substantially larger speculative blocks, yielding superlinear practical throughput gains.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control, then DFlash repeat at block=10 before testing block=11.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after block=10 DFlash best measured ~89.07 tok/s, leaving a huge same-window DFlash gap (+~95.38 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: Control check would ensure block=10 uplift is DFlash-specific in the same runtime window.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at block=10 for stability, then cautiously continue block sweep.

description: DFlash repeat at block=10 + non-spec linear cache mode reproduced at ~184.65 tok/s, confirming stable high-180 performance after control check.

asi.hypothesis: If block=10 mode is robust, repeat run should stay in the same ~184 tok/s band.
asi.next_action_hint: Update ideas to reflect new dominant block size and invalidate old block-size stale assumptions for this mode; then test block=11.

description: Adjacent block probe with `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=11` regressed to ~169.38 tok/s versus the block=10 best (~184.65), indicating the new mode peaks around 10 on this workload.

asi.hypothesis: If scaling continued past block=10 in the new non-spec cache regime, block=11 would improve further.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed materially versus current best configuration.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep block=10 as active best and avoid higher block sweeps until correctness validation is complete.

description: Retested sink under new best mode (`BLOCK_TOKENS=10`, `SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`): increasing `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK` to 56 yielded a slight new high at ~184.76 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Old sink tuning cliffs may no longer apply in the new non-spec linear cache regime, so higher sink might be beneficial.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control, then DFlash repeat to confirm whether this sink uplift is real or noise.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart for sink=56 high-throughput run measured ~89.21 tok/s, maintaining a very large same-window DFlash gap (+~95.55 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: Control parity check would confirm whether sink=56 result remains DFlash-specific in this window.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at sink=56 to determine whether the tiny sink uplift over 48 is stable or just noise.

description: Sink=56 repeat came in at ~184.33 tok/s, below the prior ~184.76 and close to sink=48 best (~184.65); indicates the sink uplift is likely noise-level, not a robust gain.

asi.hypothesis: If sink=56 was a true improvement over sink=48 in the new mode, repeat should stay above the 184.6 baseline band.
asi.rollback_reason: No repeatable primary gain versus nearby sink=48/56 samples.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat sink=48 and 56 as near-parity for now; prioritize larger levers or correctness validation over sink micro-tuning.

description: Reverted sink to 48 under block=10 + non-spec linear cache mode and hit a new best ~184.86 tok/s, indicating sink=48 is at least as good as (and likely more robust than) sink=56 in this regime.

asi.hypothesis: Given sink=56 repeat weakness, returning to sink=48 may restore stronger/stabler throughput in the new mode.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control and then DFlash repeat at this config to lock the new best.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after new best config run measured ~89.20 tok/s, maintaining a massive same-window DFlash gap (+~95.67 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: Control check would confirm the new best (~184.86) remains a DFlash-specific gain and not global drift.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at best config and then pause tuning to plan correctness validation for this regime shift.

description: Best-mode repeat (`BLOCK_TOKENS=10`, `SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`, sink=48) held at ~184.80 tok/s, confirming stable high-180 performance after control pairing.

asi.hypothesis: If the new best config is stable, repeated DFlash runs should remain near ~184.8 with low request-time variance.
asi.next_action_hint: Shift effort from micro-tuning to correctness/quality validation of this new regime before broader promotion.

description: Sink retune probe to `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=40` under best-mode settings delivered ~184.59 tok/s, essentially parity/slightly below sink=48 (~184.80). No robust sink improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Old sink cliffs may have disappeared in the new mode, so lower sink could potentially improve throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: No repeatable primary gain versus current sink=48 best.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep sink=48 as default anchor; prioritize correctness validation over further sink micro-sweeps.

description: Further block sweep to `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=12` regressed to ~173.21 tok/s from the block=10 best (~184.80), reinforcing that performance peaks near block=10 in this mode.

asi.hypothesis: If performance had a second peak at larger blocks, block=12 would recover/improve over block=10.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed materially versus current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Close >10 block sweeps for now (11 and 12 both regressive); keep block=10 as best anchor.

description: Retested mirror wrapper under the new best regime (`BLOCK_TOKENS=10`, `SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`, sink=48): enabling `DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=1` yielded a slight new high at ~184.95 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Mirror runtime wrapper may become neutral/slightly beneficial again under the new non-spec linear cache mode, despite being parity-level in older high-80 mode.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control and DFlash repeat to verify this tiny mirror-on uplift is not noise.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after mirror-on best run measured ~89.31 tok/s, keeping the same very large DFlash-over-control gap (+~95.64 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: Control check would verify whether the mirror-on high-throughput run remains DFlash-specific.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat with mirror-on config; if repeat is flat, treat mirror on/off as near-parity in the new mode too.

description: Mirror-on repeat returned ~184.74 tok/s, effectively matching prior best-mode band and erasing the tiny +0.08 uplift signal; mirror on/off appears near-parity in the new high-throughput regime.

asi.hypothesis: If mirror-on provided a real uplift in the new mode, repeat should stay measurably above non-mirror baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: No repeatable improvement; mirror-on delta is noise-scale.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat mirror on/off as equivalent again and keep focusing on correctness validation and robust config locking.

description: A/B probe with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_RECURRENT_KERNELS=0` in the new best regime returned ~184.68 tok/s, effectively parity with kernels-on (~184.74–184.95). Recurrent-kernel toggle appears low-impact when `SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`.

asi.hypothesis: If non-spec linear cache bypasses speculative recurrent hook work, recurrent-kernel toggle should become mostly irrelevant.
asi.rollback_reason: No meaningful primary gain over current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat recurrent-kernel toggle as non-critical in this mode; keep current default and focus on correctness checks.

description: Best-regime confirmation run (`MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=1`, `BLOCK_TOKENS=10`, `SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`, sink=48) remained stable at ~184.75 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: If the new configuration is robust, repeated runs should continue clustering in the ~184.7–185 range.
asi.next_action_hint: Hold this config as active best and prioritize correctness/quality validation before additional throughput sweeps.

description: Periodic explicit no-DFlash control check measured ~89.35 tok/s, consistent with recent control band and preserving a ~95 tok/s gap to current DFlash best-mode samples.

asi.hypothesis: Control floor should remain near ~89 even as DFlash high-throughput mode is stable; this guards against global benchmark drift.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue holding best DFlash config while planning/adding stronger correctness validation checks.

description: Major routing A/B in new high-throughput mode: disabling mirror default-forward override (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_MIRROR_USE_DEFAULT_TARGET_FORWARD=0`) improved to ~185.83 tok/s (new best), indicating legacy mirror target-forward path is now slightly faster in this regime.

asi.hypothesis: In the non-spec linear cache regime, mirror default-forward routing may no longer be optimal; legacy mirror forward path could reduce overhead further.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control, then DFlash repeat with this setting to verify the uplift is stable.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash counterpart after legacy-mirror-forward best run measured ~89.54 tok/s, keeping a massive same-window DFlash gap (+~96.29 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: Control parity check would confirm the `MIRROR_USE_DEFAULT_TARGET_FORWARD=0` uplift is DFlash-specific.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat with `MIRROR_USE_DEFAULT_TARGET_FORWARD=0` to verify stability before further routing tweaks.

description: DFlash repeat with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_MIRROR_USE_DEFAULT_TARGET_FORWARD=0` held high at ~185.33 tok/s, confirming this routing tweak remains above the prior ~184.75 default-forward-on band.

asi.hypothesis: If legacy mirror forward path is a real uplift in the new regime, repeat should stay above the prior default-forward-on cluster.
asi.next_action_hint: Update ideas to reflect new active best routing combo, then pause additional micro-sweeps pending correctness validation.

description: Pending interrupted probe completed: raising `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK` to 64 in the new best regime regressed to ~183.91 tok/s versus the ~185.33 anchored config.

asi.hypothesis: If additional sink depth still helped under the new mirror-forward-off regime, sink=64 would exceed the ~185.3 baseline band.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus the current validated best configuration.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert to sink=48 baseline and start the planned stability + context-length sweep triplets.

name: Qwen3.6 diagnostic: context-length scaling (prompt ~512 tokens)

description: Context-scaling characterization sample with long prompt file (~948 prompt tokens) measured DFlash at ~159.49 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Throughput should decline versus short-context (~185) as prefill work rises, but remain well above control if the new regime is robust.
asi.rollback_reason: Alternate-workload characterization run only; not a promotable optimization change.
asi.next_action_hint: Run matched explicit no-DFlash control at the same ~948-token prompt, then DFlash repeat to complete the triplet.

description: Context control run crashed immediately due malformed BENCH_PROMPT command-substitution quoting in workload.env (`python -c` received invalid escaped quotes).

asi.hypothesis: Use inline `python -c` prompt generator for context sweeps without adding prompt files to the repo.
asi.rollback_reason: Command-substitution string used escaped quote characters (`\"`) that produced invalid Python syntax.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix BENCH_PROMPT quoting to use plain Python string quotes, then rerun the ~1k prompt explicit no-DFlash control.

error_details.exception: SyntaxError: unexpected character after line continuation character

description: ~1k-prompt explicit no-DFlash control sample ended early at 187 completion tokens (EOS), so tok/s is non-comparable to fixed-1024 runs and excluded from attribution.

asi.hypothesis: Control throughput under ~1k prompt context should be measurable against DFlash when completion length is matched.
asi.rollback_reason: Early-stop/non-comparable control sample (completion_tokens != 1024).
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun the same ~1k prompt control until completion_tokens=1024, then execute matched DFlash repeat.

description: Restarted ~1k-context characterization with a length-anchored prompt (980 prompt tokens, 1024 completion tokens): DFlash measured ~176.23 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Adding explicit long-form generation instruction to the synthetic long-context prompt will eliminate early-stop artifacts and produce comparable 1024-token samples.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control at the same ~980-token prompt, then DFlash repeat to complete the attribution triplet.

description: Matched ~980-token explicit no-DFlash control (1024 completion tokens) measured ~80.31 tok/s, far below paired DFlash (~176.23).

asi.hypothesis: At longer prompt context, DFlash should retain a large throughput advantage versus explicit no-DFlash control under matched completion length.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun DFlash with identical ~980-token prompt to complete triplet and verify stability of the ~176 tok/s shelf.

description: ~980-token DFlash repeat after matched control held high at ~174.34 tok/s, confirming a stable long-context shelf well above explicit no-DFlash (~80.31).

asi.hypothesis: If the ~1k-context regime is robust, post-control DFlash rerun should remain in the mid/high-170 band with large separation from control.
asi.next_action_hint: Advance to the next context bucket (~2k prompt tokens via increased context blocks) and repeat DFlash-control-DFlash triplet.

description: Context bucket increase to ~1908 prompt tokens (range 1..64) reduced DFlash throughput to ~120.40 tok/s, as expected from higher prefill cost.

asi.hypothesis: Doubling prompt-context blocks from ~1k to ~2k tokens will materially lower completion tok/s while preserving DFlash correctness.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus current session baseline; this run is characterization for a larger context bucket.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the ~1908-token prompt shape and run explicit no-DFlash control, then DFlash repeat for matched attribution at this context length.

description: Matched explicit no-DFlash control at ~1908 prompt tokens (1024 completion tokens) measured ~76.88 tok/s, far below the paired DFlash sample (~120.40).

asi.hypothesis: Even at ~2k prompt context, DFlash should preserve substantial throughput separation over no-DFlash control.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply ~1908-token prompt with DFlash enabled and run repeat to close the triplet for this context bucket.

description: DFlash repeat at ~1908 prompt tokens reproduced at ~120.41 tok/s (essentially identical to prior ~120.40), confirming stable ~2k-context behavior and large gap vs control (~76.88).

asi.hypothesis: If the ~2k-context shelf is stable, rerun should match the initial ~120 tok/s DFlash sample closely.
asi.rollback_reason: Characterization repeat under larger context; still below current session baseline metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Return to the ~1k prompt anchor (range 1..32) and decide whether to extend sweep to ~4k context or summarize context-scaling results.

description: Extended context bucket to ~3793 prompt tokens (range 1..128): DFlash measured ~123.33 tok/s with full 1024-token completion.

asi.hypothesis: Further context growth toward ~4k tokens should reduce tok/s versus ~1k and may approach the ~2k shelf depending prefill amortization.
asi.rollback_reason: Characterization sample at larger workload bucket; metric remains below session baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Run matched explicit no-DFlash control at ~3793 prompt tokens, then DFlash repeat for same-bucket stability/attribution.

description: Matched explicit no-DFlash control at ~3793 prompt tokens (1024 completion) measured ~70.06 tok/s, far below paired DFlash (~123.33).

asi.hypothesis: DFlash should continue to outperform control at ~4k prompt context even as both degrade from short-context throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply ~3793-token prompt with DFlash enabled and run repeat to complete the long-context triplet.

description: DFlash repeat at ~3793 prompt tokens reproduced high-120 shelf (~124.30 tok/s), confirming stable long-context behavior and sustained edge over control (~70.06).

asi.hypothesis: If ~4k prompt context behavior is stable, post-control DFlash repeat should remain near the initial ~123 tok/s sample.
asi.rollback_reason: Larger-context characterization repeat remains below session baseline metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Return workload prompt range to the ~1k anchor (1..32) and summarize context-scaling findings before deciding on DDTree follow-up.

description: Post-sweep reconfirmation at ~980 prompt tokens held at ~174.18 tok/s, matching prior ~1k-context DFlash shelf after 2k/4k context excursions.

asi.hypothesis: Returning to the ~1k prompt anchor after larger-context sweeps should restore the established mid-170 DFlash shelf.
asi.next_action_hint: Use this as the context-sweep anchor; next step is either add >=8k prompt bucket or switch back to short-prompt optimization lane.

description: Switched BENCH_PROMPT back to the original short explanatory prompt (40 prompt tokens): DFlash returned to high-180 throughput (~180.54 tok/s), restoring the short-context optimization lane after context sweeps.

asi.hypothesis: Returning from long-context synthetic prompts to the original short prompt should restore the high-throughput short-context shelf.
asi.next_action_hint: Run an immediate explicit no-DFlash control counterpart on the short prompt to refresh same-window attribution after the context-sweep block.

description: Immediate short-prompt explicit no-DFlash control after restoring short-context DFlash measured ~84.14 tok/s (1024 completion), preserving a large DFlash gap versus paired ~180.54 tok/s DFlash run.

asi.hypothesis: After returning to short prompt, control should remain far below DFlash in the new non-spec cache regime.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run one more short-prompt DFlash repeat to verify the ~180 shelf is stable against this lower control window.

description: Short-prompt DFlash repeat after matched control rose to ~181.67 tok/s, confirming stable high-180 short-context performance in the restored lane.

asi.hypothesis: If restored short-context mode is stable, post-control DFlash rerun should return to high-180 throughput.
asi.next_action_hint: Context sweep is complete for ~1k/~2k/~4k buckets; decide next between >=8k context mapping or DDTree-only after explicit gating.

description: Extended context-length characterization to ~7633 prompt tokens (~8k bucket): DFlash measured ~87.97 tok/s with full 1024-token completion.

asi.hypothesis: At >=8k prompt context, DFlash throughput should drop further due to heavier prefill but still remain materially above explicit no-DFlash control.
asi.rollback_reason: Characterization run under larger workload bucket regressed versus session baseline metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the same ~7633-token prompt with explicit no-DFlash control toggles, then run DFlash repeat to complete matched attribution.

description: Matched explicit no-DFlash control at ~7633 prompt tokens (1024 completion) measured ~60.47 tok/s, well below paired DFlash (~87.97).

asi.hypothesis: Even at >=8k prompt context, DFlash should preserve a measurable throughput edge over explicit no-DFlash control.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.

asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric still below segment baseline despite quality improvement.

## 1.

1. **Core Idea...

description: Verify-cap bracket test `DFLASH_VERIFY_LEN=7` produced coherent preview output but only ~126.18 tok/s, well below the stronger coherent point at `VERIFY_LEN=8` (~164.54).

asi.hypothesis: A smaller verify cap might maintain coherence and possibly improve throughput stability versus unstable `VERIFY_LEN=9`.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed substantially versus baseline and versus better cap=8 coherent point.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat `VERIFY_LEN=8` as the best quality-safe cap seen so far; avoid further cap sweeps without deeper root-cause insight.

description: Provisional mitigation rerun with `DFLASH_VERIFY_LEN=8` again produced coherent preview output and ~165.86 tok/s in short prompt lane.

asi.hypothesis: `VERIFY_LEN=8` is a repeatable quality-safe compromise point for block=10 fast path.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remains below baseline despite improved output quality.
asi.next_action_hint: Run matched explicit no-DFlash control then DFlash repeat at `VERIFY_LEN=8` to quantify same-window quality-safe separation.

diagnostic_findings.text_preview_head: # Speculative Decoding: A detailed explanation of how speculative decoding works...

description: Matched explicit no-DFlash control for the `VERIFY_LEN=8` mitigation setup measured ~85.87 tok/s with coherent output, far below paired DFlash mitigation throughput (~165.86).

asi.hypothesis: Even with quality-safe DFlash mitigation (`VERIFY_LEN=8`), same-window control should remain near high-80 tok/s.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply DFlash `VERIFY_LEN=8` and run repeat to complete the quality-safe triplet.

description: DFlash repeat for `VERIFY_LEN=8` mitigation held at ~164.61 tok/s with coherent preview output, confirming a stable quality-safe shelf well above matched control (~85.87).

asi.hypothesis: If `VERIFY_LEN=8` is a robust mitigation, post-control DFlash rerun should reproduce mid-160 throughput with coherent text.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remains below segment baseline despite consistent quality-safe behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Document `VERIFY_LEN=8` as validated mitigation and move focus to root-cause fix that recovers >180 tok/s without output degeneration.

name: Qwen3.6 diagnostic: quality-safe short-prompt lane (verify_len=8 mitigation)

description: Baseline anchor for the re-initialized quality-safe lane (`VERIFY_LEN=8`) measured ~163.12 tok/s with coherent preview output.

asi.rollback_reason: Baseline-establishment sample for the new quality-safe optimization segment.
asi.next_action_hint: Run explicit no-DFlash control and DFlash repeat under `VERIFY_LEN=8` for same-window attribution in the new segment.

description: Quality-safe lane control sample (`VERIFY_LEN=8`, explicit no-DFlash) measured ~85.88 tok/s with coherent output.

description: Quality-safe lane DFlash repeat (`VERIFY_LEN=8`) reached ~164.05 tok/s with coherent output, improving over the new segment baseline and maintaining a large gap vs matched control (~85.88).

asi.hypothesis: The mitigated quality-safe configuration should be stable around mid-160 tok/s and still clearly outperform explicit no-DFlash control.
asi.next_action_hint: Use this as the current quality-safe anchor; next step is code-level root-cause work to recover >180 tok/s without degeneration.

description: Quality-safe lane block retune with `DFLASH_BLOCK_TOKENS=9` (keeping `VERIFY_LEN=8`) stayed coherent but regressed to ~162.09 tok/s versus block=10 anchor (~164.05).

asi.hypothesis: Lowering block size under verify-cap mitigation might preserve coherence and improve throughput stability.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus current quality-safe baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep quality-safe anchor at block=10 + verify_len=8; avoid further block downsizing unless quality evidence changes.

description: Major quality-safe improvement: increasing block size to 11 while keeping `DFLASH_VERIFY_LEN=8` delivered ~178.34 tok/s with coherent output, far above the prior quality-safe anchor (~164.05).

asi.hypothesis: With verify capped at 8, raising `BLOCK_TOKENS` above 10 may recover throughput without reintroducing degeneration, because acceptance depth remains bounded.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control, then DFlash repeat at `BLOCK_TOKENS=11` + `VERIFY_LEN=8` to confirm same-window separation and coherence stability.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash control for the quality-safe `BLOCK=11, VERIFY_LEN=8` candidate measured ~84.67 tok/s with coherent output, leaving a very large same-window DFlash gap.

asi.hypothesis: Quality-safe DFlash improvement should remain decisively above control despite mitigation overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at `BLOCK=11, VERIFY_LEN=8` to confirm stability of both throughput and output coherence.

description: DFlash repeat confirmed `BLOCK_TOKENS=11` + `VERIFY_LEN=8` quality-safe mode at ~178.27 tok/s with coherent output, sustaining a ~93.61 tok/s same-window edge over explicit control.

asi.hypothesis: If the new quality-safe configuration is robust, post-control rerun should preserve both high throughput and coherent text.
asi.next_action_hint: Promote this as the new quality-safe anchor and test one adjacent block (12) under `VERIFY_LEN=8` to see if further gain is possible without quality regression.

description: Adjacent quality-safe sweep to `BLOCK_TOKENS=12` (with `VERIFY_LEN=8`) remained coherent but regressed sharply to ~147.06 tok/s from the block=11 anchor (~178.27).

asi.hypothesis: If quality-safe throughput continues scaling with larger blocks under verify cap, block=12 might improve further.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed materially versus current quality-safe best.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep `BLOCK_TOKENS=11` + `VERIFY_LEN=8` as active quality-safe default and stop larger-block sweeps.

description: `VERIFY_LEN=9` under `BLOCK_TOKENS=11` raised throughput to ~173.69 tok/s but reintroduced catastrophic repetitive-token output (`It It It ...`), so it is rejected on quality grounds.

asi.hypothesis: Increasing verify cap above 8 might recover additional throughput in the quality-safe lane without breaking coherence.
asi.rollback_reason: Secondary metric degraded catastrophically (output coherence regression), overriding primary-metric gain.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep `VERIFY_LEN=8` as the safety boundary for block=11 and avoid higher verify caps until root cause is fixed.

asi.hypothesis: Reducing verify cap from 8 to 7 might preserve coherence and potentially improve stability without large throughput loss.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus current quality-safe anchor.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep `BLOCK=11, VERIFY_LEN=8` as active quality-safe best and stop verify-cap sweeps in this neighborhood.

description: Quality-safe routing probe with mirror wrapper disabled (`MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=0`) stayed coherent but was slightly below the current quality-safe best (~178.27–178.34 with mirror on).

asi.hypothesis: In the quality-safe `BLOCK=11, VERIFY_LEN=8` regime, disabling mirror wrapper might improve throughput while preserving coherence.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary gain over the current quality-safe best; slight regression.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep mirror wrapper enabled for the quality-safe anchor and focus on deeper root-cause/code-path fixes.

description: Quality-safe anchor reconfirm run landed at ~177.74 tok/s (coherent lane), slightly below the best observed ~178.34 and treated as noise-band stability rather than an improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Unchanged quality-safe anchor should remain in the high-177/178 shelf across reruns.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary gain over the current quality-safe best sample.
asi.next_action_hint: Proceed to code-level root-cause investigation; avoid further no-change reruns/toggle churn.

asi.rollback_reason: No meaningful primary improvement over existing quality-safe best.
