description: Quality-safe anchor rerun came in at ~177.75 tok/s, within the established high-177/178 coherent band but below the current best (~178.34).

asi.hypothesis: Unchanged quality-safe config should stay tightly clustered in the high-177/178 range.
asi.rollback_reason: No primary improvement over current quality-safe best.
asi.next_action_hint: Shift effort from reruns to code-level root-cause fixes for the uncapped fast-path degeneration.

description: Quality-safe sink retune to `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=56` stayed coherent but was slightly below the current `SINK=48` best (~178.34), so no promotable gain.

asi.hypothesis: In the `BLOCK=11, VERIFY_LEN=8` quality-safe lane, increasing sink from 48 to 56 might unlock additional throughput while preserving coherence.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed versus current quality-safe best; change is parity/noise at best.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep sink=48 as quality-safe anchor and avoid additional sink sweeps unless root-cause work changes acceptance behavior.

description: Quality-safe lane sink retune to `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=40` produced a slight new high at ~178.60 tok/s with coherent output (`BLOCK=11`, `VERIFY_LEN=8`).

asi.hypothesis: Lowering sink depth in the quality-safe configuration may reduce draft overhead while preserving coherence, yielding a small throughput uplift.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control and then DFlash repeat at `SINK=40` to confirm same-window attribution and stability.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash control counterpart for quality-safe `SINK=40` run measured ~85.72 tok/s, preserving a large same-window DFlash gap.

asi.hypothesis: Quality-safe sink retune gains should remain DFlash-specific; explicit control should stay near mid/high-80s.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at `BLOCK=11, VERIFY_LEN=8, SINK=40` to confirm stability and coherence.

description: DFlash repeat confirmed quality-safe `SINK=40` uplift at ~178.83 tok/s (`BLOCK=11`, `VERIFY_LEN=8`) with coherent output, setting a new quality-safe best.

asi.hypothesis: If sink=40 is a real quality-safe improvement, post-control DFlash rerun should meet/exceed prior ~178.60 with coherent text.
asi.next_action_hint: Update ideas to promote `SINK=40` as quality-safe default, then test one adjacent sink (e.g., 32) only if needed; otherwise pivot to code-level root-cause fix.

description: Quality-safe sink sweep continued upward: `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=32` (with `BLOCK=11`, `VERIFY_LEN=8`) reached ~179.09 tok/s while keeping coherent output.

asi.hypothesis: Further reducing sink depth in the quality-safe regime may trim draft overhead and recover additional throughput without crossing the quality boundary.
asi.next_action_hint: Run immediate explicit no-DFlash control, then DFlash repeat at `SINK=32` to validate same-window attribution and stability.

description: Immediate explicit no-DFlash control counterpart for quality-safe `SINK=32` run measured ~83.81 tok/s, preserving a very large same-window DFlash edge.

asi.hypothesis: The quality-safe sink=32 uplift should remain DFlash-specific in matched windows.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution sample only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at `BLOCK=11, VERIFY_LEN=8, SINK=32` to confirm stability and coherence before further sweeps.

description: Accuracy-drift eval-suite instrumentation attempt crashed due `set -u` array expansion bug in `autoresearch.sh` (`QUALITY_ARGS[@]` unbound).

asi.hypothesis: Add a fixed quality eval suite to harness and emit secondary METRIC lines for drift tracking each run.
asi.rollback_reason: Harness script error (`./autoresearch.sh: line 260: QUALITY_ARGS[@]: unbound variable`) caused benchmark failure.
asi.next_action_hint: Replace fragile array expansion with conditional inline args, rerun harness, then keep if stable and metric remains competitive.

description: Added fixed quality-eval suite tracking into `./autoresearch.sh` via new `scripts/siliconflash_quality_eval.py`; harness now emits `quality_eval_*` METRIC lines alongside throughput (this run ~178.06 tok/s with parsed quality metrics).

asi.hypothesis: Embedding a small deterministic eval suite into the harness will let us track accuracy/coherence drift continuously, not just spot-check previews.
asi.next_action_hint: Refine degeneracy heuristic for very short outputs (avoid false flags), then run matched control to compare quality_eval pass/degen rates DFlash vs no-DFlash.

description: Refined quality-eval degeneracy heuristic to avoid false positives on very short answers; `quality_eval_degenerate_cases` dropped from 2 to 1 while preserving detection of the long-form repetitive-loop probe.

asi.hypothesis: Length-aware degeneracy thresholds should stop incorrectly flagging concise valid answers as degenerate while retaining loop detection on long outputs.
asi.next_action_hint: Run matched explicit no-DFlash control under the same eval suite to compare pass/failure profile and isolate DFlash-specific drift signals.

diagnostic_findings.heuristic_change: short-output gating for short_token/punctuation degeneracy checks

description: Explicit no-DFlash control with the new eval suite scored perfect quality (`pass_rate=1.0`, `failed_cases=0`, `degenerate_cases=0`) at ~83.85 tok/s, highlighting quality drift in DFlash mode despite large speed gains.

asi.hypothesis: The fixed eval suite should show materially cleaner outputs for control if DFlash fast-path is introducing quality drift.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution/quality-comparison run only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat with the same suite to confirm reproducible quality gap (expected pass_rate<1 and nonzero degeneracy).

description: DFlash rerun with eval suite reproduced the quality gap (`pass_rate=0.5`, `failed_cases=2`, `degenerate_cases=1`) versus same-window control (`pass_rate=1.0`), so this high-throughput configuration is not promoted despite speed.

asi.hypothesis: If drift is DFlash-specific, rerunning DFlash after clean control should reproduce lower eval pass rate and higher degeneration metrics.
asi.rollback_reason: Catastrophic secondary-metric regression (accuracy/coherence drift) compared with control baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate root cause of failing cases (arith_addition incorrect value and coherence_probe degeneration) before any further throughput tuning claims.

description: Quality-debug probe with `BLOCK_TOKENS=8` (verify_len=8,sink=32) reduced throughput to ~147.98 tok/s and did not improve eval outcomes (`pass_rate=0.5`, arithmetic still wrong as 1010; coherence probe still degenerate).

asi.hypothesis: Lowering block size in the mitigated path might reduce drift signals on arithmetic/coherence cases.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed and quality drift persisted (no pass-rate improvement).
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on isolating arithmetic wrong-answer issue (1010 vs expected 1015) as DFlash-specific corruption rather than block-size tuning.

description: Isolation run with `DFLASH_BSTNXBT_SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1` (block11/verify8/sink32) restored perfect eval quality (`pass_rate=1.0`, `degenerate_cases=0`) but throughput collapsed to ~71.74 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Quality drift is tied to the non-spec linear cache fast path (`SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=0`); switching to speculative cache should recover quality at major speed cost.
asi.rollback_reason: Severe primary metric regression despite quality improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep quality eval enabled and explore code-path fixes that preserve spec_cache=0 speed while recovering the failing eval cases.

description: Quality-eval probe with `BLOCK_TOKENS=7` (verify_len=8,sink=32,spec_cache=0) still failed the same two cases (`arith_addition`=1010 and degenerate coherence probe) while throughput dropped to ~147.46 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Lowering block size might eliminate quality drift signatures (math error + repetition loop) in the non-spec cache path.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed heavily and quality_eval pass-rate did not improve (still 0.5).
asi.next_action_hint: Block size is not the root cause; target cache/acceptance logic around non-spec linear cache path.

description: Extreme verify-cap probe (`VERIFY_LEN=1`, block11,sink32,spec_cache0) fully restored eval quality (`pass_rate=1.0`, `degenerate_cases=0`) but throughput collapsed to ~41.99 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Quality drift may be driven by long verify spans; forcing very short verify windows should eliminate drift even in non-spec cache mode.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed catastrophically despite perfect quality metrics.
asi.next_action_hint: Quality-vs-speed tradeoff appears strongly tied to verify depth; investigate internal acceptance/commit logic for verify spans >1 instead of further brute-force cap reductions.

description: Verify-depth sweep (`VERIFY_LEN=2`) still achieved perfect eval quality (`pass_rate=1.0`) and improved over verify_len=1 speed, but remained far below baseline at ~66.90 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: A small increase from verify_len=1 may preserve quality while partially recovering throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric still massively regressed versus baseline.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue verify-depth mapping (e.g., 4) to locate the quality break point and speed jump boundary.

description: `VERIFY_LEN=4` probe was pathological: throughput collapsed to ~40.14 tok/s; quality_eval improved to pass_rate 0.75 and removed degenerate loops, but arithmetic case still failed (returned `101`).

asi.hypothesis: Mid verify caps might balance speed and quality better than extremes.
asi.rollback_reason: Severe primary regression; remaining accuracy failure persists.
asi.next_action_hint: Abandon brute-force verify_len sweeps for now and focus code-level debugging of arithmetic corruption in spec_cache=0 path.

description: `VERIFY_LEN=3` probe improved speed over very small caps (~92.90 tok/s) but quality_eval remained failing (`pass_rate=0.5`): arithmetic still wrong (`101`) and coherence probe still degenerate.

asi.hypothesis: There may be a mid verify cap that recovers substantial speed while retaining full eval quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric still below baseline and quality targets unmet.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop verify_len brute-force sweeps (1/2/3/4/7/8/9 mapped); move to code-level fix for arithmetic corruption and looping in non-spec cache path.

description: Added non-spec linear cache size knob (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_NON_SPEC_LINEAR_CACHE_SIZE=8`) and reran tuned config; throughput stayed high (~178.49 tok/s) but quality-eval remained unchanged/bad (`pass_rate=0.5`, `degenerate_cases=1`, max_run=211).

asi.hypothesis: Increasing ArraysCache size in the non-spec linear cache path may fix drift by preserving more linear history without giving up speed.
asi.rollback_reason: Catastrophic secondary-metric regression persists; no quality improvement versus prior fast-path behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Non-spec ArraysCache size is not the root cause at least up to size=8; investigate acceptance/commit token integrity instead.

description: Kernel-path isolation (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_RECURRENT_KERNELS=0`) kept high throughput (~176.44 tok/s) but quality_eval remained unchanged/failing (`pass_rate=0.5`, `degenerate_cases=1`).

asi.hypothesis: Recurrent kernel replay may be causing arithmetic/coherence drift in the fast path; disabling kernels could restore eval quality.
asi.rollback_reason: No quality improvement and slight throughput regression versus tuned config.
asi.next_action_hint: Recurrent kernel toggle is not root cause; focus on shared acceptance/verify semantics in non-spec cache path.

description: Packed-attention isolation (`PACK_TARGET=0`, `PACK_ATTENTION=0`) remained high-throughput (~176.24 tok/s) but quality_eval profile was unchanged (`pass_rate=0.5`, `degenerate_cases=1`).

asi.hypothesis: Packed target/attention hooks might be introducing token corruption in the fast path; disabling them could improve eval outcomes.
asi.rollback_reason: No quality improvement and no primary gain over tuned config.
asi.next_action_hint: Packed-path toggles are not the quality root cause; keep them enabled and continue investigating shared acceptance/verify logic.

description: Exit-layer retune (`DFLASH_MIRROR_SD_EXIT_LAYER=20`) slightly increased throughput (~179.39 tok/s) but left quality_eval unchanged at `pass_rate=0.5` with the same two failing cases.

asi.hypothesis: Using a deeper mirror exit layer might improve quality drift while preserving high throughput.
asi.rollback_reason: Catastrophic secondary-metric regression persists; quality did not improve despite primary uplift.
asi.next_action_hint: Exit-layer depth is not the quality lever in this regime; deprioritize mirror exit sweeps for drift recovery.

description: Quantified mirror-wrapper isolation with eval suite: `MIRROR_SD_RUNTIME=0` held high throughput (~179.33 tok/s) but quality remained identical/failing (`pass_rate=0.5`, same two failed cases).

asi.hypothesis: If mirror wrapper itself is responsible for drift, disabling it should improve eval outcomes.
asi.rollback_reason: Catastrophic secondary-metric regression unchanged; no quality improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Mirror wrapper is not root cause; keep focusing on shared fast-path acceptance/commit behavior.

description: Quality-first path improvement: `SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1` with `RECURRENT_KERNELS=0` kept perfect eval quality (`pass_rate=1.0`) and improved speed to ~82.14 tok/s (up from ~71.74 with kernels on), but still far below baseline.

asi.hypothesis: In the speculative-linear-cache path, recurrent replay kernels may be a major overhead; disabling them could recover throughput while preserving quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remains severely regressive versus baseline despite quality improvements.
asi.next_action_hint: If pursuing quality-first fallback, this is a better sub-configuration than kernels-on; otherwise continue root-cause work to recover quality on the fast path.

description: Telemetry sweep point (fast path, context~45 prompt tokens): captured acceptance baseline at short context (`acceptance_rate=0.679`, `full_accept_rate=0.008`, `mean_commit_tokens=7.76`). Diagnostic-only run for curve mapping.

asi.hypothesis: At short context, fast-path acceptance should remain relatively high before long-context collapse appears.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic telemetry point only; not an optimization candidate and quality eval disabled for sweep speed.
asi.next_action_hint: Increase prompt context to ~1k/~2k/~4k/~8k and measure acceptance curve under same config.

description: Telemetry sweep point (fast path, context~1k prompt tokens): acceptance dipped versus short context (`acceptance_rate=0.662`, `mean_commit_tokens=7.59`) with throughput down to ~159.69 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Acceptance and throughput start dropping once prompt context crosses ~1k tokens.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic telemetry point only; no candidate optimization change.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue sweep at ~2k/~4k/~8k to map acceptance slope and verify/eval cost growth.

description: Telemetry sweep point (fast path, context~2k prompt tokens): throughput dropped to ~141.74 tok/s while acceptance stayed around ~0.664 and mean commit stayed ~7.59.

asi.hypothesis: Beyond ~1k context, much of the slowdown may come from growing prefill/verify cost rather than a large acceptance collapse.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic telemetry point only; no optimization candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Probe 4k/8k contexts to confirm whether acceptance remains relatively flat while latency components scale with context.

description: Telemetry sweep point (fast path, context~4k prompt tokens): acceptance dropped further to ~0.628 and throughput fell to ~110.84 tok/s; prefill cost rose sharply (`prefill_s~2.58`).

asi.hypothesis: Past ~4k context, acceptance degradation becomes material and combines with prefill scaling to produce large tok/s loss.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic telemetry point only; no optimization candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Run ~8k point to complete context curve and compare acceptance slope vs prefill scaling.

description: Telemetry sweep point (fast path, context~8k prompt tokens): throughput collapsed to ~80.15 tok/s with very large prefill cost (`prefill_s~5.69`); acceptance stayed around ~0.67 in this sample.

asi.hypothesis: At very long context, throughput loss is dominated by context-dependent compute (especially prefill), with acceptance contributing but not solely responsible.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic telemetry point only; no optimization candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Run matching context sweep under `SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1` to isolate cache-policy contribution to acceptance behavior.

description: Spec-cache reference sweep attempt at short context was invalid for curve comparison: run stopped early (`completion_tokens=135`, `finish_reason=stop`), so acceptance metrics are not comparable to length-capped fast-path runs.

asi.hypothesis: Spec-cache path would provide a cleaner acceptance reference across context lengths.
asi.rollback_reason: Non-comparable sample due early EOS stop; needs forced-length rerun.
asi.next_action_hint: Rerun spec-cache sweep with `IGNORE_EOS=1` so all points hit comparable completion length.

description: Telemetry reference point (`SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1`, `IGNORE_EOS=1`, short context~45): acceptance is much lower than fast mode (`acceptance_rate=0.359`, `mean_commit_tokens=4.57`) with throughput ~55.69 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Spec-cache-safe path sacrifices acceptance depth/commit size for stability, explaining large speed gap vs fast mode.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic reference point only; no optimization candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue spec-cache reference sweep at ~1k/~2k/~4k/~8k contexts for acceptance-vs-context curve.

description: Telemetry reference point (`SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1`, `IGNORE_EOS=1`, context~1k): acceptance stayed low (`0.355`) with mean commit ~4.53; throughput ~64.19 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Spec-cache acceptance remains in a lower band across contexts relative to fast mode, with smaller commit spans.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic reference point only; no optimization candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue 2k/4k/8k points for full spec-cache acceptance curve.

description: Telemetry reference point (`SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1`, `IGNORE_EOS=1`, context~2k) showed severe acceptance collapse (`acceptance_rate=0.0606`, `mean_commit_tokens=1.60`) with throughput ~20.04 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Spec-cache path may hit a hard acceptance cliff around this context regime on this prompt family.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic reference point only; no optimization candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Run 4k/8k points to determine if this is a monotonic collapse or a context-dependent instability pocket.

description: Telemetry reference point (`SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1`, `IGNORE_EOS=1`, context~4k) remained in the severe-collapse regime (`acceptance_rate=0.0607`, `mean_commit_tokens=1.60`) with ~20.39 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Spec-cache lane entered a persistent low-acceptance basin around >=2k context for this workload.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic reference point only; no optimization candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Run final ~8k point to confirm whether collapse persists or recovers at very long context.

description: Telemetry reference point (`SPECULATIVE_LINEAR_CACHE=1`, `IGNORE_EOS=1`, context~8k): still low-acceptance regime (`acceptance_rate=0.0859`, `mean_commit_tokens=1.85`) with throughput ~21.12 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Spec-cache acceptance collapse beyond ~2k is persistent but not strictly monotonic; slight recovery can occur at very long context while remaining far below short-context levels.
asi.rollback_reason: Diagnostic reference point only; no optimization candidate.
asi.next_action_hint: Synthesize both mode curves and prioritize code-level investigation around acceptance/commit semantics in fast path.

description: Added verify warmup cap support (`DFLASH_VERIFY_LEN_WARMUP_STEPS=8`, `DFLASH_VERIFY_LEN_WARMUP_CAP=2`) on top of `VERIFY_LEN=8`: throughput stayed high (~173.56 tok/s) while quality_eval improved from 0.5 to 0.75 (arith fixed, no degenerate cases).

asi.hypothesis: Constrain verify depth only for the first draft steps to protect early-token correctness, then return to high-throughput verify span.

diagnostic_findings.coherence_preview_head: # Speculative Decoding: A Detailed Explanation ... acceleratesLLLLLLLLLLLL

description: Same-window explicit no-DFlash control for warmup-cap candidate: ~86.00 tok/s with perfect quality_eval (`pass_rate=1.0`, no degeneracy), maintaining large separation from DFlash candidate.

asi.hypothesis: The warmup-cap DFlash candidate should remain clearly above an explicit no-DFlash control in the same window.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution run only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat with warmup cap config to complete triplet and verify quality/pass-rate stability.

description: Triplet completion run for warmup-cap config: DFlash repeat stayed high (~175.56 tok/s) with the same improved quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`), confirming large same-window edge vs control.

asi.hypothesis: The warmup-cap candidate should reproduce high throughput and the improved 3/4 quality suite profile on repeat.
asi.rollback_reason: Attribution repeat only; no new code change in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Tune warmup schedule (`steps`/`cap`) to target full quality pass (1.0) while retaining >170 tok/s.

description: Warmup-depth sweep (`VERIFY_LEN_WARMUP_STEPS=16`, cap=2) regressed throughput (~160.09 tok/s) and worsened coherence stability (degenerate_cases back to 1) while pass_rate stayed 0.75.

asi.hypothesis: Extending low-verify warmup duration might fully stabilize coherence without losing much speed.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed and secondary quality did not improve (degeneracy returned).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep warmup window short; test stronger cap (`warmup_cap=1`) with short warmup steps instead.

description: Stronger warmup cap (`VERIFY_LEN_WARMUP_CAP=1`, steps=8) kept high speed (~175.45 tok/s) but coherence regressed (degenerate_cases=1, max_run=240), so no net quality gain over cap=2.

asi.hypothesis: Using cap=1 during warmup might further suppress early drift and recover full quality suite pass.
asi.rollback_reason: Secondary quality worsened versus kept warmup-cap baseline (`degenerate_cases` rose from 0 to 1).
asi.next_action_hint: Revert to warmup cap=2 and test a narrower warmup window (e.g., 4 steps) to preserve coherence gains without over-constraining.

description: Narrow warmup window (`VERIFY_LEN_WARMUP_STEPS=4`, cap=2) recovered high speed (~178.07 tok/s) but lost coherence protection (degenerate_cases=1; repeat-loop probe returned), so no quality improvement.

asi.hypothesis: Shorter warmup might preserve throughput while keeping most of the quality gains from warmup-cap logic.
asi.rollback_reason: Secondary quality regressed versus kept warmup baseline (`degenerate_cases` increased from 0 to 1).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep `warmup_steps=8, warmup_cap=2` as current best tradeoff; investigate targeted coherence-case guard rather than global warmup retunes.

description: Retuned to `VERIFY_LEN=9` while keeping warmup guard (`WARMUP_STEPS=8`, `WARMUP_CAP=2`): recovered high throughput (~181.86 tok/s) and preserved improved quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`).

asi.hypothesis: Warmup-limited verify can stabilize early-token correctness enough to safely run a larger steady-state verify cap for higher speed.

description: Same-window explicit no-DFlash control for warmup+verify9 candidate measured ~85.61 tok/s with perfect quality_eval (`pass_rate=1.0`).

asi.hypothesis: verify9+warmup DFlash gains should remain clearly above explicit no-DFlash control in matched conditions.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution run only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat with verify9+warmup config to complete triplet and verify stability.

description: Triplet completion run for verify9+warmup config reproduced throughput (~181.92 tok/s) and quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`), confirming large same-window edge vs control.

asi.hypothesis: verify9+warmup candidate should remain stable on repeat after explicit control.
asi.rollback_reason: Attribution repeat only; no new code change in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Target remaining coherence failure (non-degenerate but malformed long-token output) with case-specific guard/instrumentation.

description: Block retune under warmup-guarded verify9 (`BLOCK_TOKENS=10`) remained at pass_rate 0.75 with no degeneracy, but throughput dropped to ~174.60 tok/s vs block11 (~181.9).

asi.hypothesis: Lowering block size under verify9+warmup might eliminate the remaining coherence failure while keeping good speed.
asi.rollback_reason: No quality improvement and significant primary regression versus current verify9+warmup anchor.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep block=11 for this lane; remaining failure likely needs token-content guard or deeper acceptance semantics fix.

description: Added periodic verify resync (`RESYNC_INTERVAL=16`, `RESYNC_CAP=2`) on top of verify9+warmup; quality profile stayed unchanged (`pass_rate=0.75`, no degeneracy) and throughput regressed to ~177.01 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Periodic short verify pulses might prevent the remaining coherence corruption while preserving most of verify9+warmup speed.
asi.rollback_reason: No quality improvement and meaningful primary regression versus verify9+warmup anchor (~181.9).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep warmup-only guard; if revisiting resync, profile with telemetry first and test looser intervals/caps.

description: Warmup-guarded verify9 retune with `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=40` improved throughput to ~182.92 tok/s while preserving the improved quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`).

asi.hypothesis: In the verify9+warmup regime, sink=40 may recover additional speed without undoing quality gains from warmup limiting.

description: Same-window explicit no-DFlash control after sink=40 keep measured ~86.65 tok/s with perfect quality_eval (`pass_rate=1.0`, no failures).

asi.hypothesis: The sink=40 DFlash candidate should maintain a large same-window edge versus explicit no-DFlash control.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution run only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at sink=40 to complete triplet and confirm stability.

description: Triplet completion run for sink=40 verify9+warmup config reproduced ~182.39 tok/s with same quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`), confirming large same-window edge vs control.

asi.hypothesis: Sink=40 verify9+warmup configuration should be stable on repeat after explicit control.
asi.rollback_reason: Attribution repeat only; no new code/config change in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Investigate remaining coherence-case failure with targeted runtime instrumentation around token commits in long continuation.

description: Added repeat-token rescue guard (`REPEAT_RUN_TRIGGER=12`, `REPEAT_RUN_STEPS=8`, `REPEAT_RUN_CAP=2`) on top of verify9+warmup; throughput collapsed to ~76.27 tok/s with no quality gain (`pass_rate=0.75`).

asi.hypothesis: Detecting long same-token runs and temporarily shrinking verify cap could break coherence collapse while preserving speed.
asi.rollback_reason: Severe primary regression with unchanged quality outcome.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not enable repeat-token rescue in current form; if revisited, use telemetry to verify activation frequency and much looser trigger/shorter rescue.

description: Rollback-copy fallback probe (`DFLASH_BSTNXBT_ROLLBACK_SNAPSHOT_COPY=1`) stayed near baseline speed (~182.89 tok/s) but did not improve quality (`pass_rate=0.75`, same single coherence failure).

asi.hypothesis: Reference snapshot copy mode might eliminate subtle cache-state drift causing the remaining coherence failure.
asi.rollback_reason: No quality improvement and no meaningful primary gain versus current sink40 verify9+warmup anchor.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat rollback snapshot copy as non-root-cause in this regime; continue investigating acceptance/commit semantics.

description: Raised steady-state verify cap to 10 (warmup unchanged at 8/2): throughput regressed to ~175.46 tok/s with no quality gain (`pass_rate=0.75`, one remaining failure).

asi.hypothesis: A slightly larger steady-state verify cap might improve the remaining coherence behavior after warmup stabilization.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed substantially versus verify9+warmup anchor with unchanged quality outcome.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep verify_len=9 in warmup-guarded lane; remaining failure is not fixed by larger verify cap.

description: Warmup-cap loosened to 3 (`VERIFY_LEN=9`, warmup steps=8): throughput remained high (~180.05) but quality regressed back to bad profile (`pass_rate=0.5`, `degenerate_cases=1`).

asi.hypothesis: A slightly looser warmup cap might retain speed while still protecting coherence.
asi.rollback_reason: Catastrophic secondary regression versus warmup-cap=2 (quality dropped from 0.75/deg0 to 0.5/deg1).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep warmup cap=2 as strict boundary; do not loosen without additional safeguards.

description: Warmup length retune to 10 steps (cap=2) preserved the improved quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, degenerate=0) but was slightly slower (~181.80 tok/s) than 8-step anchor.

asi.hypothesis: A slightly longer warmup window might improve stability of the remaining coherence case without hurting speed.
asi.rollback_reason: No quality improvement and slight primary regression versus warmup-8 anchor.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep warmup_steps=8 as best observed tradeoff; focus on different mechanism for final coherence failure.

description: Sink retune in warmup-guarded verify9 lane: `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=56` improved throughput to ~183.80 tok/s while preserving current quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`).

asi.hypothesis: Higher sink may improve throughput in verify9+warmup regime without reintroducing degeneracy.

description: Same-window explicit no-DFlash control after sink=56 keep measured ~87.48 tok/s with perfect quality_eval (`pass_rate=1.0`, no failures).

asi.hypothesis: Sink=56 warmup-verify9 DFlash candidate should maintain a large same-window edge versus explicit control.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution run only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at sink=56 to complete triplet and confirm stability.

description: Triplet completion run for sink=56 verify9+warmup config reproduced high throughput (~183.04 tok/s) with unchanged quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`), confirming large same-window edge vs control.

asi.hypothesis: Sink=56 candidate should remain stable on repeat after explicit no-DFlash control.
asi.rollback_reason: Attribution repeat only; no new code/config change in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Move from sink sweeps to targeted fix for the final coherence-case failure.

description: Extended sink sweep in verify9+warmup lane: `DFLASH_DRAFT_SINK=64` improved throughput further to ~184.27 tok/s while preserving current quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`).

asi.hypothesis: Higher sink depth might continue improving throughput in warmup-guarded verify9 mode without reintroducing degeneracy.

description: Same-window explicit no-DFlash control after sink=64 keep measured ~87.03 tok/s with perfect quality_eval (`pass_rate=1.0`).

asi.hypothesis: Sink=64 verify9+warmup DFlash candidate should maintain strong same-window separation versus explicit control.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution run only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat at sink=64 to complete triplet and validate stability.

description: Triplet completion run for sink=64 verify9+warmup config remained high (~182.78 tok/s) with unchanged quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`).

asi.hypothesis: Sink=64 candidate should remain stable on repeat after explicit control.
asi.rollback_reason: Attribution repeat only; no new change in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue root-cause work on the last coherence failure instead of further sink sweeps.

description: Kernel-path toggle in current warmup-verify9/sink64 lane (`RECURRENT_KERNELS=0`) was near-parity/slightly lower (~183.55 tok/s) with unchanged quality (`pass_rate=0.75`, no degeneracy).

asi.hypothesis: Disabling recurrent kernels might improve final coherence behavior in the new warmup-guarded regime.
asi.rollback_reason: No quality improvement and slight primary regression versus current best config.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep recurrent kernels enabled; this toggle remains non-root-cause.

description: Enhanced quality eval diagnostics: each case now records `token_count` and optional `dflash_usage` telemetry fields. Confirmed failing coherence case has low token_count (~16) with moderate acceptance (~0.55), not classic repeat-loop metrics; throughput remained high (~183.81 tok/s).

asi.hypothesis: Per-case token-count and dflash-usage diagnostics will expose whether the remaining coherence failure is acceptance-depth collapse or a different commit/text-integrity mode.

description: Same-window explicit no-DFlash control after diagnostics keep: ~85.39 tok/s with perfect quality_eval (`pass_rate=1.0`, no failures).

asi.hypothesis: Instrumentation-only changes should not alter control behavior; control should stay in expected mid/high-80 band with clean quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Control attribution run only.
asi.next_action_hint: Run DFlash repeat with sink64 verify9+warmup to complete triplet.

description: Triplet completion run after diagnostics keep: DFlash remained high (~180.91 tok/s) with unchanged quality profile (`pass_rate=0.75`, `degenerate_cases=0`).

asi.hypothesis: Post-control DFlash repeat should stay on the high-throughput shelf with same quality profile.
asi.rollback_reason: Attribution repeat only; no new code/config change in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Use new per-case diagnostics to target the low-token-count coherence failure path in runtime logic.

description: Block-down retune (`BLOCK_TOKENS=9`) in sink64 verify9+warmup lane reduced throughput to ~169.23 tok/s with no quality gain (still pass_rate 0.75, one coherence failure).

asi.hypothesis: Reducing speculative block size might eliminate the remaining low-token-count coherence failure.
asi.rollback_reason: Significant primary regression with unchanged quality outcome.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep block=11 in this lane; final failure likely needs content-integrity guard rather than block downsizing.

description: Context-sweep setup probe with synthetic repeated-token prompt hit early EOS (`completion_tokens=27`), making tok/s non-comparable for DFlash vs control scaling.

asi.hypothesis: Simple repeated-token prompt could be used to anchor exact prompt-token length for context sweep.
asi.rollback_reason: Early-stop sample is invalid for fixed-workload tok/s comparison.
asi.next_action_hint: Use an explanatory suffix prompt (long-form generation anchor) when padding context so completion reaches full 1024 tokens.

description: Context sweep point (DFlash best config) at prompt_tokens=40 with QUALITY_EVAL disabled for throughput-only comparison: ~181.06 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Using current best DFlash config at short prompt should reproduce ~high-180 tok/s shelf for sweep baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Measurement-only run for user-requested DFlash vs control context curve.
asi.next_action_hint: Run matched explicit no-DFlash control at same prompt length and compute gap.

description: Context sweep control run failed before launch because workload referenced missing `.omlx-autoresearch/bench_prompt_dynamic.txt`.

asi.hypothesis: Run matched no-DFlash control at prompt_tokens~40 using dynamic prompt file.
asi.rollback_reason: Harness failed immediately due missing BENCH_PROMPT source file.
asi.next_action_hint: Recreate bench_prompt_dynamic.txt then rerun the control sample.
asi.error_detail: cat: .omlx-autoresearch/bench_prompt_dynamic.txt: No such file or directory

description: Context sweep matched explicit no-DFlash control at prompt_tokens=40 (QUALITY_EVAL=0): ~84.48 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Control baseline at 40 prompt tokens should stay in mid-80 tok/s band in same-window comparison.
asi.rollback_reason: Measurement-only control sample for user-requested DFlash-vs-control gap table.
asi.next_action_hint: Advance to ~1024 prompt-token pair with same best DFlash config and explicit control toggle.

description: Context sweep point (DFlash best config) at prompt_tokens=1024 with QUALITY_EVAL disabled: ~142.53 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: Best DFlash config should retain strong edge at ~1k prompt tokens with expected context-scaling slowdown.
asi.rollback_reason: Measurement-only sweep point.
asi.next_action_hint: Run matched no-DFlash control at prompt_tokens~1024.

description: Context sweep matched explicit no-DFlash control at prompt_tokens=1024 (QUALITY_EVAL=0): ~83.40 tok/s.

asi.hypothesis: No-DFlash control at ~1k prompt tokens should stay around low/mid-80 tok/s and below DFlash shelf.
